Thus, Job passed away, having lived a long and full life.
Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, Nor stand in the path of sinners, Nor sit in the seat of scoffers!
Rather, he finds joy in the Lord's Law, And he reflects on His Law both day and night.
He is like a tree planted by streams of water, Which bears its fruit in its season, And its leaf remains green; And in all that he undertakes, he thrives.
The wicked are not like this, But they resemble chaff that the wind drives away.
Thus, the wicked will not endure in the judgment, Nor will sinners be found in the gathering of the righteous.
For the Lord is aware of the path of the righteous, But the path of the wicked leads to ruin.
Why do the nations rage, And the peoples devise futile plans?
The kings of the earth take their stand, And the rulers join forces Against the Lord and His Anointed, saying,
“Let us break their chains, And cast their cords from us!”
He who dwells in the heavens laughs, The Lord mocks them.
Then He will address them in His anger, And alarm them in His wrath, saying,
“Yet I have set My King On Zion, My sacred hill.”
I will declare the decree of the Lord: He said to Me, “You are My Son, Today I have become Your Father.
Request of Me, and I will surely grant the nations as Your inheritance, And the farthest parts of the earth as Your possession.
You shall crush them with a rod of iron, You shall dash them to pieces like pottery.”
Therefore, you kings, be wise; Be open to instruction, you judges of the earth.
Worship the Lord with awe, And celebrate with trembling.
Honour the Son, lest He be angry and you perish in your path, For His wrath can ignite swiftly.
Blessed are all who seek refuge in Him. A Psalm of David, when he fled from his son Absalom. Lord, how my enemies have increased! Many are rising up against me.
Many are saying about me, “There is no deliverance for him through God.” Selah
Yet You, Lord, are a shield around me, My glory, and the One who lifts my head high.
I called out to the Lord with my voice, And He responded to me from His sacred mountain. Selah
I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the Lord upholds me.
I will not fear the multitudes of people Who have encircled me.
Rise up, Lord; deliver me, my God! For You have struck all my foes on the cheek; You have broken the teeth of the wicked.
Deliverance is the Lord's; May Your blessing rest upon Your people.
Selah For the music director; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Answer me when I call, God of my righteousness! You have relieved me in my distress; Be gracious to me and hear my prayer.
You sons of man, how long will my honour be turned into shame? How long will you love what is worthless and strive for a lie? Selah
Yet understand that the Lord has chosen the faithful for Himself; The Lord listens when I call to Him.
Be in awe, and do not sin; Reflect quietly in your heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah
Present offerings of righteousness, And place your trust in the Lord.
Many are saying, “Who will show us any good?” Shine the light of Your face upon us, Lord!
You have filled my heart with joy, More than when their grain and new wine overflow.
I will lie down and sleep in peace, For You alone, Lord, provide me with safety.
For the music director; for flutes. A Psalm of David. Listen to my words, Lord, Consider my sighing.
Hear my plea for help, my King and my God, For it is to You I pray.
In the morning, Lord, You will hear my voice; At dawn, I will present my prayer to You and keep watch.
For You are not a God who delights in wickedness; No evil can reside with You.
The boastful will not stand before Your eyes; You despise all who commit injustice.
You bring to ruin those who speak falsehoods; The Lord detests the person of bloodshed and deceit.
But as for me, by Your abundant mercy I will enter Your house; At Your holy temple I will bow in reverence for You.
Lord, guide me in Your righteousness because of my foes; Make Your path straight before me.
For there is nothing reliable in their speech; Their inner being is destruction itself. Their throat is an open grave; They use their tongue to flatter.
Hold them accountable, God; Let them fall by their own schemes! In the multitude of their transgressions, drive them out, For they have rebelled against You.
But let all who seek refuge in You be glad; Let them ever sing for joy! And may You protect them, That those who love Your name may exult in You.
For You bless the righteous person, Lord, You surround them with favour as with a shield.
For the music director; with stringed instruments, upon an eight-string lyre. A Psalm of David. Lord, do not rebuke me in Your anger, Nor discipline me in Your wrath.
Show kindness to me, Lord, for I am weak; Heal me, Lord, for my bones are in distress.
And my soul is deeply troubled; But You, Lord—how long?
Return, Lord, deliver my soul; Save me because of Your loving kindness.
For there is no remembrance of You in death; In Sheol, who will give You praise?
I am exhausted with my groaning; Every night I drench my bed, I soak my couch with my tears.
My eye has grown weak with sorrow; It has aged because of all my foes.
Depart from me, all you who commit injustice, For the Lord has heard the sound of my weeping.
The Lord has listened to my plea, The Lord accepts my prayer.
All my enemies will be put to shame and greatly troubled; They shall turn back, they will suddenly be put to shame.
A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the Lord concerning Cush, a Benjaminite. Lord my God, in You I have taken refuge; Save me from all those who pursue me, and rescue me,
Or he will tear my soul like a lion, Dragging me away, while there is no one to rescue.
Lord my God, if I have done this, If there is injustice in my hands,
If I have done evil to my ally, Or have plundered my enemy for no reason,
Allow the enemy to pursue my soul and overtake it; And let him trample my life to the ground And lay my honour in the dust. Selah
Rise, Lord, in Your anger; Stand against the fury of my enemies, And awaken for me; You have appointed judgment.
Let the gathering of the peoples surround You, And return to Your place on high.
The Lord judges the peoples; Judge me, Lord, according to my righteousness and the integrity within me.
Let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; For the righteous God examines hearts and minds.
My protection is with God, Who delivers those who are upright in heart.
God is a fair judge, And a God who expresses displeasure every day.
If one does not repent, He will sharpen His sword; He has bent His bow and made it ready.
He has also prepared His deadly weapons; He makes His arrows fiery shafts.
Behold, an evil person is filled with injustice, And he conceives harm and gives birth to falsehood.
He has dug a pit and made it deep, And has fallen into the hole he created.
His harm will return upon his own head, And his violence will come down on his own crown.
I will thank the Lord for His righteousness And will sing praise to the name of the Lord Most High.
For the music director; on the Gittith. A Psalm of David. Lord, our Lord, How majestic is Your name across the earth, You who have displayed Your splendour above the heavens!
From the mouths of infants and nursing babies, You have ordained strength Because of Your adversaries, To silence the foe and the avenger.
When I reflect on Your heavens, the work of Your hands, The moon and the stars, which You have positioned,
What is humanity that You are mindful of them, And whats is a child of man that You care for him?
Yet You have made them a little lower than the divine, And You crown them with glory and honour!
You have given them dominion over the works of Your hands; You have placed everything under their feet,
All sheep and oxen, And also the creatures of the field,
The birds of the air, and the fish of the sea, Whatever travels the paths of the seas.
Lord, our Lord, How majestic is Your name across the earth!
For the music director; on Muth-labben. A Psalm of David. I will give thanks to the Lord with all my heart; I will tell of all Your wonders.
I will rejoice and be joyful in You; I will sing praises to Your name, O Most High.
When my enemies retreat, They stumble and perish in Your presence.
For You have upheld my just cause; You have sat on the throne giving righteous judgment.
You have reproved the nations, You have brought an end to the wicked; You have wiped out their name for all time.
The enemy has met an end in everlasting ruins, And You have uprooted the cities; The very memory of them has vanished.
Yet the Lord reigns as King forever; He has established His throne for judgment,
And He will judge the world with righteousness; He will govern the peoples with fairness.
The Lord will also be a refuge for the oppressed, A stronghold in times of trouble;
And those who know Your name will trust in You, For You, Lord, have not forsaken those who seek You.
Sing praises to the Lord, who resides in Zion; Proclaim His deeds among the peoples.
For He who avenges blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
Be gracious to me, Lord; Observe my suffering from those who hate me, You who lift me from the gates of death,
So that I may recount all Your praises, That in the gates of the daughter of Zion I may rejoice in Your salvation.
The nations have sunk into the pit they made; In the net they hid, their own foot is ensnared.
The Lord has revealed Himself; He has executed judgment. A wicked one is trapped in the work of his own hands. Higgaion Selah
The wicked will descend to Sheol, Along with all the nations that forget God.
For the needy will not always be forgotten, Nor will the hope of the afflicted vanish forever.
Arise, Lord, do not let mankind triumph; Let the nations be judged in Your presence.
Instil fear in them, Lord; Let the nations know they are merely human.
Selah Why do You seem distant, Lord? Why do You conceal Yourself in times of trouble?
In their arrogance, the wicked fervently pursue the needy; Let them be ensnared in the schemes they have devised.
For the wicked boasts of his heart’s desire, And the greedy person curses and shows contempt for the Lord.
The wicked, in his pride, does not seek Him. There is no room for God in all his plans.
His ways prosper at all times; Yet Your judgments are beyond his reach; As for all his enemies, he sneers at them.
He says to himself, “I shall not be shaken; Through all generations I shall not face adversity.”
His mouth is full of curses, deceit, and oppression; Under his tongue are mischief and injustice.
He waits in ambush in the villages; He kills the innocent in hidden places; His eyes secretly watch for the unfortunate.
He lies in wait like a lion in his den; He waits to seize the needy; He seizes the needy when he draws him into his net.
He crouches, he bows down, And the unfortunate fall by his strong ones.
He says to himself, “God has overlooked; He has hidden His face; He will never notice.”
Rise up, Lord; God, lift Your hand. Do not overlook the humble.
Why has the wicked shown contempt for God? He has said to himself, “You will not call me to account.”
You have observed it, for You have noted harm and provocation to take it into Your hand. The unfortunate entrusts himself to You; You have been the helper of the orphan.
Break the power of the wicked and the evildoer, Seek out his wickedness until none remains.
The Lord reigns as King forever and ever; Nations have vanished from His land.
Lord, You have heard the longing of the humble; You will strengthen their heart, You will incline Your ear,
To uphold the orphan and the oppressed, So that mankind, who is of the earth, will no longer cause fear.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. In the Lord I take refuge; How can you say to my soul, “Flee as a bird to your mountain;
For, observe, the wicked prepare their bow, They have readied their arrow on the string To shoot in the shadows at those upright in heart.
If the very foundations are destroyed, What action can the righteous take?”
The Lord is in His holy temple; The Lord’s throne is in heaven; His eyes behold, His eyelids test the sons of mankind.
The Lord examines both the righteous and the wicked, And He deeply dislikes one who delights in violence.
He will rain coals of fire upon the wicked, Fire and brimstone and burning wind will be the portion of their cup.
For the Lord is just, He cherishes righteousness; Those who are upright will behold His face.
For the music director; upon an eight- stringed lyre. A Psalm of David. Help, Lord, for the godly person has come to an end, For the faithful have disappeared from the sons of mankind.
They speak deceitfully to one another; They use flattering lips and harbour duplicity in their hearts.
May the Lord silence all flattering lips, The tongue that boasts of great things;
Those who have said, “With our words, we will succeed; Our lips belong to us; who can be master over us?”
“Because of the oppression of the poor, because of the sighing of the needy, Now I will arise,” says the Lord; “I will place him in the safety he desires.”
The words of the Lord are pure words; Like silver refined in a furnace on the earth, Purified seven times.
You, Lord, will preserve them; You will guard him from this generation perpetually.
The wicked prowl on every side, When depravity is honoured among the sons of mankind.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. How long, Lord? Will You forget me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
How long will I carry this concern in my soul, With sorrow in my heart throughout the day? How long will my enemy triumph over me?
Look upon me and respond, O Lord my God; Brighten my eyes, lest I fall into the sleep of death,
And my enemy will declare, “I have prevailed against him,” And my adversaries will be glad when I am unsettled.
Yet I have placed my trust in Your steadfast love; My heart shall find joy in Your deliverance.
I will sing to the Lord, Because He has been gracious to me.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, they have committed detestable acts; There is no one who does good.
The Lord gazes from heaven upon the realms of men To see if there are any who are wise, Who seek after God.
They have all turned away, together they have become corrupt; There is no one who does good, not even one.
Are the workers of injustice unaware, Who consume my people as they eat bread, And do not call upon the Lord?
There they are, filled with great fear, For God is with the generation of the righteous.
You seek to frustrate the plans of the poor, But the Lord is their refuge.
Oh, that deliverance for Israel would come from Zion!
When the Lord brings back the fortunes of His people, Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad. A Psalm of David. Lord, who may reside in Your tent? Who may settle on Your holy hill?
A person who lives with integrity, acts justly, And speaks truthfully from the heart.
He does not slander with his tongue, Nor do evil to his neighbour, Nor bring shame on his friend;
In his eyes a despicable person is despised, But he honours those who fear the Lord; He takes an oath to his own detriment, and does not change;
He does not lend his money unfairly, Nor does he accept a bribe against the innocent.
Whoever lives in this manner will remain steadfast. A Mikhtam of David. Protect me, God, for I take refuge in You.
I declared to the Lord, “You are my Lord; I have no goodness apart from You.”
Regarding the saints who are on the earth, They are the noble ones; all my delight is in them.
The sorrows of those who pursue another god will be multiplied; I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood, Nor will I speak their names with my lips.
The Lord is my portion and my cup of blessing; You uphold my destiny.
The boundary lines have fallen for me in pleasant places; Indeed, I have a delightful inheritance.
I will praise the Lord who guides me; Indeed, my heart instructs me in the night.
I continually set the Lord before me; With Him at my right hand, I shall not be shaken.
Therefore my heart is glad and my spirit rejoices; My body also will rest securely.
For You will not leave my soul in Sheol; You will not let Your Holy One see decay.
You will show me the path of life; In Your presence is the fullness of joy; At Your right hand are pleasures for evermore.
A Prayer of David. Hear a just cause, Lord, give Your attention to my cry; Listen to my prayer, which is not from deceitful lips.
Let my judgment come from Your presence; Let Your eyes look with integrity.
You have put my heart to the test; You have visited me by night; You have examined me and found nothing; My intent is not to transgress with my mouth.
As for the deeds of mankind, By the word of Your lips I have kept from the paths of the violent.
I have remained steadfast in Your ways. My feet have not faltered.
I have called upon You, for You will respond to me, God; Incline Your ear to me, heed my words.
Show Your wonderful faithfulness, You who save those who take refuge at Your right hand From those who rise up against them.
Guard me as the cherished one; Shelter me under the cover of Your wings
From the wicked who act violently against me, My mortal foes who encircle me.
They have hardened their unfeeling hearts, With their mouths they speak arrogantly.
They have now encircled us in our steps; They set their eyes to bring us down to the ground.
He is like a lion that is eager to tear, And like a young lion lurking in secret places.
Arise, Lord, face him, bring him low; Deliver my soul from the wicked with Your sword,

From people by Your hand, Lord, From people of the world, whose portion is in this life, And whose belly You fill with Your treasure; They are satisfied with children, And leave their abundance to their infants.
As for me, I shall gaze upon Your face in righteousness; I shall be fulfilled with Your likeness when I awake.

For the music director. A Psalm of David, the servant of the Lord, who spoke to the Lord the words of this song on the day that the Lord rescued him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul. And he said: I love You, Lord, my strength.
The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my Saviour, My God, my rock in whom I take refuge; My shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
I call upon the Lord, who is worthy of praise, And I am delivered from my enemies.
The cords of death encircled me, And the torrents of destruction filled me with dread.
The grips of the grave entangled me; The traps of death confronted me.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, And cried to my God for assistance; He heard my voice from His temple, And my cry for help reached His ears.
Then the earth trembled and quaked; And the foundations of the mountains shook And were moved, because He was angry.
Smoke rose from His nostrils, And consuming fire came from His mouth; Burning coals blazed forth.
He also bent the heavens and descended, With thick darkness under His feet.
He rode upon a cherub and flew; And He soared on the wings of the wind.
He made darkness His secret place, His covering around Him, Dark waters, thick clouds of the skies.
From the brightness before Him His thick clouds passed, Hailstones and coals of fire.
The Lord also thundered in the heavens, And the Most High raised His voice, Hailstones and coals of fire.
He sent out His arrows and scattered them, And abundant lightning flashes, and routed them.
Then the channels of water were seen, And the foundations of the world were laid bare By Your rebuke, Lord, At the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
He reached down from on high, He took hold of me; He drew me out of deep waters.
He delivered me from my powerful enemy, And from those who hated me, for they were too strong for me.
They confronted me on the day of my calamity, But the Lord was my support.
He also brought me out into a spacious place; He rescued me, because He delighted in me.
The Lord has rewarded me in accordance with my righteousness; According to the purity of my hands He has repaid me.
For I have followed the ways of the Lord, And have not turned away from my God.
For all His judgments were before me, And I did not disregard His statutes.
I was also blameless before Him, And I kept myself from wrongdoing.
Therefore the Lord has repaid me in accordance with my righteousness, According to the purity of my hands in His sight.
With the faithful You show Yourself faithful; With the blameless You reveal Yourself blameless;
With the pure You show Yourself pure, And with the devious You show Yourself shrewd.
For You save a humble people, But You bring down haughty eyes.
For You light my lamp; The Lord my God brightens my darkness.
For with Your help I can run against a troop; And with my God I can leap over a wall.
As for God, His way is perfect; The word of the Lord is tried; He is a shield to all who seek refuge in Him.
For who is God, if not the Lord? And who is a rock, if not our God?
The God who arms me with strength, And makes my way perfect?
He makes my feet nimble like those of a deer, And sets me securely on the heights.
He prepares my hands for battle, So that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
You have also given me the shield of Your salvation, And Your right hand sustains me; And Your gentleness exalts me.
You broaden the path beneath me, And my feet have not slipped.
I pursued my enemies and overtook them, And I did not turn back until they were destroyed.
I struck them down, so that they could not rise; They fell beneath my feet.
For You have equipped me with strength for battle; You have subdued those who rose up against me.
You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me, And I annihilated those who hated me.
They cried for help, but there was no one to save them; They cried to the Lord, but He did not respond to them.
Then I pulverised them like dust before the wind; I cast them out like mud in the streets.
You delivered me from the disputes of the people; You made me the leader of the nations; A people I had not known serve me.
As soon as they hear, they obey me; Foreigners yield to me.
Foreigners lose heart, And come trembling from their strongholds.
The Lord lives, and blessed be my rock; And exalted be the God who saves me,
The God who brings justice to me, And subdues peoples under me.
He delivers me from my enemies; You indeed lift me above those who rise up against me; You rescue me from a violent man.
Therefore I will express my gratitude to You among the nations, Lord, And I will sing praises to Your name.
He grants great salvation to His king, And shows steadfast love to His anointed, To David and his descendants forever.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. The heavens tell of the glory of God; And their expanse declares the work of His hands.
Each day brings forth speech, And each night unveils knowledge.
They do not speak, nor do they use words; Their voice remains unheard.
Their message has gone out into all the earth, And their words to the end of the world. In them He has set a dwelling for the sun,
Which is like a bridegroom emerging from his chamber; It delights like a strong runner to follow its path.
Its rising is from one end of the heavens, And its circuit to the other end of them; And nothing is hidden from its warmth.
The Law of the Lord is flawless, reviving the soul; The testimony of the Lord is certain, imparting wisdom to the simple.
The precepts of the Lord are just, bringing joy to the heart; The commandment of the Lord is clear, giving light to the eyes.
The reverence for the Lord is pure, lasting forever; The judgments of the Lord are trustworthy; they are altogether righteous.
They are more precious than gold, even much fine gold; Sweeter also than honey and the drippings of the honeycomb.
Furthermore, Your servant is enlightened by them; In observing them there is great reward.
Who can recognize his own errors? Cleanse me from hidden faults.
Also restrain Your servant from wilful sins; Let them not dominate me; Then I will be blameless, And free from great transgression.
May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be pleasing in Your sight, Lord, my rock and my Redeemer.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. May the Lord answer you on a day of trouble! May the name of the God of Jacob protect you!
May He send you assistance from the sanctuary, And uphold you from Zion!
May He remember all your offerings And regard your burnt sacrifices with favour! Selah
May He grant you your heart’s desire And fulfil all your plan!
We will sing for joy over your victory, And in the name of our God we will set up our banners. May the Lord fulfil all your requests.
Now I am certain that the Lord delivers His anointed; He will respond to him from His holy heaven With the powerful strength of His right hand.
Some praise chariots and some horses, But we will praise the name of the Lord, our God.
They have collapsed and fallen, But we have risen and stand firm.
Deliver us, Lord; May the King respond to us on the day we call.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. Lord, the king will rejoice in Your strength, And how greatly he will rejoice in Your salvation!
You have granted him his heart’s desire, And You have not denied the request of his lips. Selah
For You welcome him with the blessings of good things; You place a crown of pure gold upon his head.
He asked You for life, and You granted it to him, Length of days for all time.
His glory is great through Your salvation, Splendour and majesty You bestow upon him.
For You make him exceedingly blessed forever; You fill him with joy in Your presence.
For the king has faith in the Lord, And through the steadfastness of the Most High he will remain unshaken.
Your hand will reach all your enemies; Your right hand will seize those who bear hatred towards you.
You will make them like a blazing furnace in the time of your anger; The Lord will consume them in His wrath, And fire will engulf them.
You will remove their descendants from the earth, And their offspring from among the children of mankind.
Though they plotted evil against You And devised a scheme, They will not prevail.
For You will make them retreat; You will aim at their faces with Your bowstrings.
Be lifted high, Lord, in Your strength; We will sing and praise Your might.
For the music director; upon Aijeleth Hashshahar. A Psalm of David. My God, my God, why have You forsaken me? Far from my help are the words of my groaning.
My God, I call out by day, yet You do not respond; And by night, but I find no peace.
Yet You are holy, You who sit enthroned upon the praises of Israel.
In You our ancestors placed their trust; They trusted, and You delivered them.
To You they cried out and found refuge; In You they trusted and were not put to shame.
But I am like a worm, not a man, A reproach among people and despised by the populace.
All who see me mock me; They sneer, they shake their heads, saying,
“Commit yourself to the Lord; let Him deliver him; Let Him rescue him, since He delights in him.”
Yet it was You, the One who brought me forth from the womb; You made me trust while on my mother’s breasts.
From birth, I was entrusted to You; You have been my God since my mother’s womb.
Do not be distant from me, for trouble is near; For there is no one to assist.
Many bulls have surrounded me; Mighty bulls of Bashan have encircled me.
They gape at me with their mouths, Like a ravenous and roaring lion.
I am poured out like water, And all my bones are out of their joints; My heart is like wax; It has melted within me.
My strength is dried up like a shard of pottery, And my tongue clings to my jaws; And You lay me in the dust of death.
For dogs have surrounded me; Like a pack, evildoers encircle me; They have pierced my hands and my feet.
I can count all my bones. They look, they gaze upon me;
They share my garments among them, And they draw lots for my clothing.
But You, Lord, do not be far away; You who are my help, rush to my aid.
Deliver my soul from the sword, My precious life from the grasp of the dog.
Rescue me from the lion’s mouth; From the horns of the wild oxen, You have answered me.
I will declare Your name to my brethren; In the midst of the congregation, I will praise You.
You who revere the Lord, praise Him; All you descendants of Jacob, honour Him, And stand in awe of Him, all you descendants of Israel.
For He has not disregarded nor abhorred the suffering of the afflicted; Nor has He hidden His face from him; But when he cried to Him for help, He listened.
From You comes my praise in the great congregation; I will fulfil my vows before those who revere Him.
The afflicted will eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise the Lord. May your heart endure forever!
All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the Lord, And all the families of the nations will bow before You.
For the kingdom belongs to the Lord, And He governs the nations.
All the prosperous of the earth will eat and worship, All those who descend to the dust will kneel before Him, Even he who cannot sustain his own life.
Future generations will serve Him; It will be recounted of the Lord to the coming generation.
They will come and declare His righteousness To a people yet to be born, that He has accomplished it.
A Psalm of David. The Lord is my shepherd, I will not want.
He makes me lie down in the green pastures; He leads me beside quiet waters.
He refreshes my soul; He leads me along the paths of righteousness For His name's sake.
Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I shall fear no evil, for You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me.
You set a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You have blessed my head with oil; My cup is overflowing.
Certainly goodness and mercy will follow me all the days of my life, And I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.
A Psalm of David. The earth is the Lord’s, and all it contains, The world, and those who live in it.
For He has set it upon the seas And made it firm upon the waters.
Who is permitted to ascend the hill of the Lord? And who is able to stand in His holy place?
The one with clean hands and a pure heart, Who has not turned his soul to falsehood And has not sworn falsely.
Such a person will receive a blessing from the Lord And righteousness from the God who saves him.
This is the generation of those who seek Him, Who seek Your face—Jacob. Selah
Raise your heads, you gates, And rise up, you ancient doors, That the King of glory may enter!
Who is the King of glory? The Lord, strong and mighty, The Lord, powerful in battle.
Raise your heads, you gates, And lift them up, you ancient doors, That the King of glory may enter!
Who is this majestic King?
The Lord of hosts, He is truly the King of glory. Selah A Psalm of David. To You, Lord, I lift up my soul.
My God, in You I trust, Do not let me be disgraced; Do not allow my enemies to triumph over me.
Truly, none of those who wait for You will be disgraced; Those who act treacherously without reason will be put to shame.
Reveal Your ways to me, Lord; Guide me in Your paths.
Direct me in Your truth and instruct me, For You are the God of my salvation; I wait for You all day long.
Remember, Lord, Your mercy and Your steadfast love, For they have existed from ancient times.
Do not recall the sins of my youth or my transgressions; Remember me according to Your steadfast love, For the sake of Your goodness, Lord.
The Lord is kind and just; Therefore He guides sinners in the right path.
He leads the humble in what is right, And He teaches the humble His way.
All the ways of the Lord are steadfast love and truth To those who keep His covenant and His decrees.
For the honour of Your name, Lord, Pardon my iniquity, for it is vast.
Who is the one who reveres the Lord? He will guide him in the path he should select.
His soul will abide in well-being, And his offspring will inherit the land.
The counsel of the Lord is for those who revere Him, And He will reveal to them His covenant.
My eyes are ever on the Lord, For He will free my feet from the snare.
Turn to me and show me favour, For I am isolated and troubled.
The anxieties of my heart have multiplied; Deliver me from my distresses.
Consider my affliction and my trouble, And pardon all my sins.
Observe my foes, for they are numerous, And they bear a fierce hatred against me.
Protect my soul and deliver me; Do not let me be disgraced, for I seek refuge in You.
May integrity and uprightness guard me, For I place my hope in You.
Deliver Israel, God, From all his troubles.
A Psalm of David. Vindicate me, Lord, for I have walked in my integrity, And I have trusted in the Lord without wavering.
Search me, Lord, and try my heart; Purify my thoughts and my soul.
For Your steadfast love is before my eyes, And I have followed Your truth.
I do not associate with deceitful people, Nor will I join with pretenders.
I detest the gathering of evildoers, And I will not sit with the wicked.
I will cleanse my hands in innocence, And I will encircle Your altar, Lord,
So that I may proclaim with a voice of gratitude And declare all Your marvellous deeds.
Lord, I cherish the abode of Your house, And the place where Your glory dwells.
Do not gather my soul with sinners, Nor my life with those who shed blood,
In whose hands are wicked schemes, And whose right hand is full of bribes.
But as for me, I will walk in my integrity; Rescue me, and show me Your favour.
My foot stands on solid ground; In the congregations, I will praise the Lord.
A Psalm of David. The Lord is my light and my salvation; Whom should I fear? The Lord is the defence of my life; Whom should I dread?
When evildoers approached me with intent to harm, My adversaries and my enemies, they stumbled and fell.
Though an army encamps against me, My heart will not fear; Though war arises against me, Even then I am confident.
One thing I have requested from the Lord, this I will seek: That I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, To gaze upon the beauty of the Lord And to reflect in His temple.
For in the day of trouble He will shelter me in His tabernacle; He will hide me in the secret place of His tent; He will set me high upon a rock.
And now my head will be exalted above my enemies around me, And I will offer sacrifices in His tent with joyful shouts; I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to the Lord.
Hear me, Lord, when I cry with my voice, And be gracious to me and respond to me.
When You said, “Seek My face,” my heart responded to You, “I will seek Your face, Lord.”
Do not hide Your face from me, Do not reject Your servant in anger; You have been my help; Do not abandon me nor forsake me, God of my salvation!
Though my father and my mother have forsaken me, The Lord will receive me.
Teach me Your way, Lord, And guide me on a level path because of my enemies.
Do not deliver me to the will of my adversaries, For false witnesses have risen against me, And the violent breathe out violence.
I am confident that I will see the goodness of the Lord In the land of the living.
Wait for the Lord; Be strong and let your heart take courage; Indeed, wait for the Lord.
A Psalm of David. To You, Lord, I call; My rock, do not be deaf to me, For if You are silent to me, I will become like those who go down to the pit.
Listen to my pleas when I cry out for Your help, When I lift my hands toward Your sacred sanctuary.
Do not drag me away with the wicked And with those who practice injustice, Who speak peace with their neighbours, While evil is in their hearts.
Repay them according to their deeds And according to the wickedness of their actions; Repay them for the work of their hands; Give them what they deserve.
Because they do not consider the works of the Lord Nor the deeds of His hands, He will tear them down and not build them up.
Blessed be the Lord, For He has listened to the voice of my pleas.
The Lord is my strength and my shield; My heart trusts in Him, and I am helped; Therefore my heart rejoices, And with my song I shall thank Him.
The Lord is the strength of His people, And He is a stronghold of salvation for His anointed.
Deliver Your people and grant favour to Your heritage; Guide them and support them always.
A Psalm of David. Ascribe to the Lord, sons of the mighty, Ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
Ascribe to the Lord the glory due His name; Worship the Lord in holy splendour.
The voice of the Lord echoes over the waters; The God of glory roars, The Lord reigns over the vast waters.
The voice of the Lord is full of power, The voice of the Lord is full of majesty.
The voice of the Lord shatters the cedars; Indeed, the Lord splinters the cedars of Lebanon.
He causes Lebanon to leap like a calf, And Sirion to bound like a young wild ox.
The voice of the Lord divides forks of fire.
The voice of the Lord quakes the wilderness; The Lord causes the wilderness of Kadesh to tremble.
The voice of the Lord prompts the deer to give birth And lays the forests bare; And in His temple, all cry, “Glory!”
The Lord was enthroned as King at the flood; Indeed, the Lord reigns as King forever.
The Lord will impart strength to His people; The Lord will bestow peace upon His people.
A Psalm; a Song at the Dedication of the House. A Psalm of David. I will exalt You, Lord, for You have lifted me up, And have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
O Lord my God, I called to You for assistance, and You restored me.
Lord, You have raised my soul from Sheol; You have preserved my life, so that I would not descend to the pit.
Offer praise to the Lord, you His faithful ones, And give thanks at the mention of His holiness.
For His anger is but for a moment, His favour is for a lifetime; Weeping may last for the night, But a shout of joy comes in the morning.
Now as for me, I said in my prosperity, “I shall never be shaken.”
Lord, by Your favour You have made my mountain to stand strong; You hid Your face, I was dismayed.
To You, Lord, I called, And I pleaded with the Lord:
“What benefit is there in my demise, if I descend to the pit? Will the dust give You praise? Will it proclaim Your faithfulness?
Listen, Lord, and show me Your grace; Lord, be my support.”
You have transformed my mourning into dancing; You have removed my sackcloth and surrounded me with joy,
So that my soul may sing praise to You and not remain silent.
O Lord my God, I will express my gratitude to You forever. For the music director. A Psalm of David. In You, Lord, I have taken refuge; Let me never be put to shame; In Your righteousness rescue me.
Turn Your ear to me, deliver me without delay; Be a steadfast rock for me, A fortress to protect me.
For You are my rock and my stronghold; For the sake of Your name, You will lead and guide me.
You will free me from the net they have secretly set for me, For You are my strength.
Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have redeemed me, Lord, faithful God.
I reject those who cling to worthless idols, But I trust in the Lord.
I will rejoice and be glad in Your favour, Because You have seen my misery; You have known the troubles of my soul,
And You have not handed me over to the enemy’s hand; You have set my feet in a large place.
Show me kindness, Lord, for I am in distress; My eyes are worn out from grief, my soul and my body as well.
For my life is consumed with sorrow And my years with sighing; My strength has failed because of my guilt, And my body has withered away.
Because of all my adversaries, I have become a disgrace, Especially to my neighbours, And an object of dread to my acquaintances; Those who see me in the street flee from me.
I am forgotten like someone who is dead, out of mind; I am like a shattered vessel.
For I have heard the whispering of many, “Terror on every side!” As they conspired together against me, They plotted to take my life.
Yet I trust in You, Lord, I declare, “You are my God.”
My times are in Your hand; Deliver me from the grasp of my enemies and from those who pursue me.
Let Your face shine upon Your servant; Save me through Your faithfulness.
Lord, do not let me be put to shame, for I call upon You; Let the wicked be put to shame, let them be silent in Sheol.
Let the deceitful lips be silenced, Which speak arrogantly against the righteous With pride and contempt.
How abundant is Your goodness, Which You have reserved for those who fear You, Which You have accomplished for those who seek refuge in You, In the sight of humanity!
You hide them in the shelter of Your presence from the schemes of people; You keep them safe in a refuge from the strife of tongues.
Blessed be the Lord, For He has shown His marvellous faithfulness to me in a besieged city.
In my alarm, I said, “I am cut off from Your sight!” Yet You heard the sound of my pleadings When I called to You for help.
Love the Lord, all His faithful ones! The Lord preserves the loyal But fully repays the one who acts arrogantly.
Be strong and let your heart be courageous, All you who hope in the Lord.
A Psalm of David. A Maskil. Blessed is the one whose wrongdoing is forgiven, Whose sin is covered.
Blessed is the person whose guilt the Lord does not take into account, And in whose spirit there is no deceit.
When I kept silent about my wrongdoing, my body grew weak Through my constant groaning.
For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; My strength was drained as in the heat of summer. Selah
I acknowledged my wrongdoing to You, And I did not conceal my guilt; I said, “I will confess my transgressions to the Lord”; And You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah
Therefore, let everyone who is godly pray to You while You may be found; Surely in a flood of great waters, they will not reach him.
You are my refuge; You protect me from trouble; You surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah
I will guide you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with My watchful eye upon you.
Do not be like the horse or the mule, which lack understanding, Whose gear includes bit and bridle to restrain them, Otherwise they will not come near to you.
The sorrows of the wicked are many, But the one who trusts in the Lord will be surrounded by goodness.
Be joyful in the Lord and rejoice, you righteous ones, And shout for joy, all you who are upright in heart.
Rejoice in the Lord, you righteous; Praise is fitting for the upright.
Offer thanks to the Lord with the lyre; Sing to Him with a harp of ten strings.
Sing to Him a new song; Play skillfully with joyful shouts.
For the word of the Lord is true, And all His work is done with faithfulness.
He cherishes righteousness and justice; The earth is filled with the goodness of the Lord.
By the word of the Lord the heavens were created, And by the breath of His mouth all their host.
He gathers the waters of the sea as a heap; He stores the depths in storehouses.
Let the whole earth revere the Lord; Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
For He spoke, and it came into existence; He gave the command, and it was established.
The Lord thwarts the counsel of nations; He frustrates the schemes of peoples.
The counsel of the Lord endures forever, The thoughts of His heart through all generations.
Fortunate is the nation whose God is the Lord, The people He has chosen as His own possession.
The Lord observes from heaven; He sees all the children of humanity;
From His dwelling place He watches All the inhabitants of the earth,
He who shapes the hearts of them all, He who comprehends all their deeds.
A king is not saved by a large army; A warrior is not delivered by great strength.
A horse is a vain hope for victory; Nor does it deliver by its great strength.
Behold, the eye of the Lord is on those who revere Him, On those who hope in His faithfulness,
To deliver their soul from death And to sustain them in famine.
Our soul awaits the Lord; He is our help and our shield.
For our heart delights in Him, Because we trust in His holy name.
Let Your favour, Lord, be upon us, Just as we have hoped in You.
A Psalm of David, when he pretended to be insane before Abimelech, who drove him away and he departed. I will continually praise the Lord; His praise will always be on my lips.
My soul will boast in the Lord; The humble will hear and be glad.
Exalt the Lord with me, And let us exalt His name together.
I sought the Lord, and He responded to me, And delivered me from all my fears.
They looked to Him and were radiant, And their faces will never be covered with shame.
This humble man cried out, and the Lord listened to him, And rescued him from all his troubles.
The angel of the Lord surrounds those who revere Him, And delivers them.
Experience and see that the Lord is good; How fortunate is the one who takes refuge in Him!
Revere the Lord, you His holy ones; For those who revere Him lack nothing.
The young lions may grow weak and hungry; But those who seek the Lord lack no good thing.
Come, my children, listen to me; I will teach you the reverence of the Lord.
Who is the person who desires life And loves many days, that he may see good?
Guard your speech from evil And your lips from uttering deceit.
Turn away from evil and do good; Seek peace and strive to maintain it.
The Lord watches over the righteous, And His ears are attentive to their cry.
The face of the Lord is against those who do evil, To erase their memory from the earth.
The righteous call out, and the Lord hears them, And delivers them from all their troubles.
The Lord is near to those who are broken-hearted And saves those who are crushed in spirit.
The righteous may have many troubles, But the Lord delivers him from them all.
He guards all his bones, Not one of them will be broken.
Calamity will overtake the wicked, And those who hate the righteous will be held accountable.
The Lord redeems the souls of His servants, And none of those who take refuge in Him will be condemned.
A Psalm of David. Contend, Lord, with those who contend with me; Fight against those who fight against me.
Grasp the shield and armour, And stand up to assist me.
Draw the spear and javelin to face those who pursue me; Assure my soul, “I am your salvation.”
Let those be ashamed and dishonoured who seek my life; Let those be turned back and humiliated who devise evil against me.
Let them be like chaff before the wind, With the angel of the Lord driving them onward.
Let their path be dark and slippery, With the angel of the Lord pursuing them.
For they set their net for me without reason; Without reason they dug a pit for my soul.
Let destruction come upon him when he is unaware, And let the net he hid ensnare him; Let him fall into that very destruction.
And my soul shall rejoice in the Lord; It shall rejoice in His salvation.
All my bones will declare, “Lord, who is like You, Who rescues the afflicted from one who is too strong for him, And the afflicted and the poor from one who robs him?”
False witnesses rise up; They question me about things I do not know.
They return harm for my good, Bringing grief to my soul.
But as for me, when they were sick, I wore sackcloth; I humbled my soul with fasting, But my prayer returned unanswered.
I went about as though it were my friend or brother; I bowed down in mourning, like one who grieves for a mother.
But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered themselves together; The afflicted whom I did not know gathered together against me, They slandered me without ceasing.
Like irreverent jesters at a feast, They gnashed at me with their teeth.
Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue my soul from their ravages, My precious life from the lions.
I will give You thanks in the great congregation; I will praise You among a vast assembly.
Do not let those who are unjustly my enemies rejoice over me; Nor let those who hate me without cause wink maliciously.
For they do not speak of peace, But they devise deceitful words against those who live quietly in the land.
They opened their mouths wide against me; They exclaimed, “Aha, aha! Our eyes have witnessed it!”
You have seen it, Lord, do not remain silent; Lord, do not be distant from me.
Rouse Yourself, and awake to my right And to my cause, my God and my Lord.
Judge me, Lord my God, according to Your righteousness, And prevent them from rejoicing over me.
Do not let them say in their heart, “Aha, our desire!” Do not let them say, “We have consumed him!”
Let those be ashamed and altogether humiliated who rejoice at my distress; Let those be clothed with shame and dishonour who exalt themselves over me.
Let them shout for joy and rejoice, who take delight in my vindication; And let them continually say, “The Lord be exalted, Who delights in the prosperity of His servant.”
And my tongue shall proclaim Your righteousness And Your praise throughout the day.
For the music director. A Psalm of David the servant of the Lord. Wrongdoing speaks to the ungodly within his heart; There is no fear of God before his eyes.
For it flatters him in his own eyes Concerning the discovery of his guilt and hatred.
His words are full of wickedness and deceit; He has abandoned wisdom and goodness.
He devises evil while on his bed; He chooses a path that is not good; He does not turn away from wrongdoing.
Your lovingkindness, Lord, reaches to the heavens, Your faithfulness extends to the skies.
Your righteousness is as majestic as the mountains; Your judgments are as profound as the great deep. Lord, You care for both humans and animals.
How invaluable is Your lovingkindness, God! And humanity finds shelter under the shadow of Your wings.
They are satisfied with the abundance of Your house; And You refresh them with the river of Your delights.
For with You is the source of life; In Your light, we perceive light.
Continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You, And Your righteousness to those with upright hearts.
Let not the foot of arrogance overtake me, And let not the hand of the wicked force me away.
There the wrongdoers have fallen; They have been cast down and cannot rise.
A Psalm of David. Do not get upset because of evildoers, Do not be envious of wrongdoers.
For they will soon fade like the grass, And wither like green plants.
Place your trust in the Lord and do good; Dwell in the land and nurture faithfulness.
Delight yourself in the Lord; And He will fulfil the desires of your heart.
Entrust your path to the Lord, Rely on Him, and He will act.
He will make your righteousness shine like the dawn, And your justice like the noonday sun.
Be still before the Lord and wait patiently for Him; Do not be troubled by those who succeed in their ways, By those who carry out wicked schemes.
Refrain from anger and forsake wrath; Do not be upset, it leads only to harm.
For evildoers will be cut off, But those who hope in the Lord will inherit the land.
In a little while, the wicked will be no more; Though you look for them, they will not be found.
But the meek will inherit the land, And will enjoy abundant peace.
The wicked devise schemes against the righteous, And gnash their teeth at them.
The Lord laughs at the wicked, For He knows their day is coming.
The wicked prepare their weapons To bring down the poor and needy, To harm those who walk uprightly.
Their swords will pierce their own hearts, And their bows will be broken.
A little that the righteous have Is better than the wealth of many wicked.
For the strength of the wicked will be broken, But the Lord supports the righteous.
The Lord watches over the days of the blameless, And their inheritance will endure forever.
They will not be put to shame in times of trouble, And in days of famine they will have abundance.
But the wicked will perish; The enemies of the Lord will vanish like the splendour of the meadows, They will disappear like smoke.
The wicked borrow and do not repay, But the righteous are generous and give.
Those blessed by Him will inherit the land, But those cursed by Him will be cut off.
The Lord makes firm the steps of the one who delights in Him; He takes pleasure in their way.
Though they stumble, they will not fall, For the Lord upholds them with His hand.
I was young and now I am old, Yet I have never seen the righteous forsaken Or their children begging for bread.
They are always generous and lend freely, And their children will be a blessing.
Turn from evil and do good, So that you may dwell in the land forever.
For the Lord loves justice And will not forsake His faithful ones; They will be protected forever, But the offspring of the wicked will be cut off.
The righteous will inherit the land And live in it perpetually.
The mouths of the righteous speak wisdom, And their tongues express what is just.
The law of their God is in their hearts; Their steps do not falter.
The wicked lie in wait for the righteous, Seeking to take their lives.
But the Lord will not abandon them to the power of the wicked Or let them be condemned when brought to trial.
Hope in the Lord and follow His path, And He will lift you up to inherit the land; When the wicked are destroyed, you will see it.
I have seen a wicked and ruthless person Flourishing like a luxuriant native tree.
But they soon passed away and were no more; Though I looked for them, they could not be found.
Consider the blameless, observe the upright; A future awaits those who pursue peace.
But all who sin will be destroyed; There will be no future for the wicked.
The salvation of the righteous comes from the Lord; He is their stronghold in times of trouble.
The Lord helps them and delivers them; He rescues them from the wicked and saves them, Because they seek refuge in Him.
A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance. Lord, do not rebuke me in Your wrath, And do not punish me in Your burning anger.
For Your arrows have pierced me deeply, And Your hand has weighed heavily upon me.
There is no healthy spot in my flesh because of Your indignation; There is no health in my bones because of my sin.
For my wrongdoings have overwhelmed me; Like a heavy burden they are too much for me to bear.
My wounds are unpleasant and inflamed Due to my own folly.
I am stooped and deeply bowed down; I walk in mourning all day long.
For my sides are filled with burning, And there is no healthy spot in my flesh.
I feel faint and utterly crushed; I groan because of the turmoil in my heart.
Lord, all my longing is before You; And my sighing is not hidden from You.
My heart beats wildly, my strength fails me; And the light of my eyes, even that has left me.
My loved ones and my friends keep their distance from my affliction, And my relatives stand far away.
Those who seek my life set traps for me; And those who seek to harm me talk of destruction, And plot deception all day long.
But I, like one who is deaf, do not hear; And I am like one who cannot speak, who does not open his mouth.
Yes, I am like one who does not hear, And in whose mouth there are no arguments.
For I wait for You, Lord; You will respond, Lord my God.
For I said, “Let them not rejoice over me, Who, when my foot slips, would exalt themselves over me.”
For I am on the verge of falling, And my sorrow is ever before me.
For I confess my guilt; I am filled with anxiety because of my sin.
But my enemies are vigorous and strong, And those who unjustly hate me are many.
And those who repay evil for good, They oppose me, because I pursue what is good.
Do not forsake me, Lord; My God, do not be distant from me!
Hasten to assist me, Lord, my salvation!

For the music director, for Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said, “I will keep watch over my ways So that I do not sin with my tongue; I will keep watch over my mouth as with a muzzle While the wicked are in my presence.”
I was mute and silent, I refrained even from good, And my pain was stirred.
My heart burned within me, While I was reflecting, the fire ignited; Then I spoke with my tongue:
“Lord, help me understand my end, And the measure of my days; Let me realise how fleeting I am.
Behold, You have made my days as short as hand spans, And my lifetime is as nothing before You; Truly, all humanity is but a breath. Selah
Truly, every person moves like a passing shadow; They make a commotion for no purpose; He gathers wealth, not knowing who will collect it.
And now, Lord, what am I to anticipate? My hope rests in You.
Deliver me from all my transgressions; Do not let me be a scorn to the foolish.
I have become silent, I do not open my mouth, Because it is You who have acted.
Remove Your affliction from me; Because of the pressure of Your hand, I am undone.
With rebukes You discipline a person for iniquity; You consume what is dear to him like a moth; Truly, all humanity is but a breath. Selah
Hear my prayer, Lord, and listen to my cry for help; Do not ignore my tears; For I am a stranger with You, A wanderer like all my ancestors.
Turn Your gaze from me, that I may find joy again Before I depart and am no more.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the Lord; And He reached down to me and heard my cry.
He lifted me from the pit of ruin, out of the mire; And He set my feet upon a rock, making my steps secure.
He placed a new song in my mouth, a hymn of praise to our God; Many will see and stand in awe, And will place their trust in the Lord.
Blessed is the man who has made the Lord his trust, And has not turned to the proud, nor to those who become involved in falsehood.
Numerous, Lord my God, are the wonders You have performed, And Your thoughts toward us; There is none to compare with You. If I were to declare and speak of them, They would be beyond counting.
You have not desired sacrifices and offerings; You have opened my ears; You have not required burnt offerings and sin offerings.
Then I said, “Look, I have come; It is written about me in the scroll of the book.
I take joy in doing Your will, my God; Your Law is within my heart.”
I have announced the good news of righteousness in the great assembly; Behold, I will not hold back my lips, Lord, You know.
I have not hidden Your righteousness within my heart; I have declared Your faithfulness and Your salvation; I have not concealed Your mercy and Your truth from the great congregation.
You, Lord, will not withhold Your compassion from me; Your mercy and Your truth will continually guard me.
For evils beyond number have surrounded me; My guilty deeds have overtaken me, so that I cannot see; They are more numerous than the hairs of my head, And my heart has failed me.
Be pleased, Lord, to deliver me; Make haste, Lord, to assist me.
May those be ashamed and humiliated together Who seek my life to destroy it; May those be turned back and dishonoured Who delight in my hurt.
May those be dismayed because of their shame Who say to me, “Aha, aha!”
May all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; May those who love Your salvation continually say, “The Lord be magnified!”
But I am afflicted and needy; May the Lord be mindful of me.
You are my help and my saviour; Do not delay, my God. For the music director. A Psalm of David. Blessed is one who considers the helpless; The Lord will save him on a day of trouble.
The Lord will watch over him and preserve his life, And he will be regarded as blessed upon the earth; And do not hand him over to the will of his enemies.
The Lord will support him on his sickbed; In his illness, You restore him to health.
As for me, I said, “Lord, show mercy to me; Heal my soul, for I have sinned against You.”
My enemies speak ill of me, “When will he die, and his name vanish?”
And when he comes to see me, he speaks insincerely; His heart harbours wickedness; When he goes outside, he spreads it.
All who despise me whisper together against me; They devise harm against me, saying,
“A dire affliction has seized him, So that when he lies down, he will not rise again.”
Even my close friend in whom I trusted, Who shared my bread, Has turned against me.
But You, Lord, show mercy to me and raise me up, That I may repay them.
By this I know that You are pleased with me, Because my enemy does not triumph over me.
But as for me, You sustain me in my integrity, And You place me in Your presence forever.
Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, From eternity to eternity.
Amen and Amen. For the music director. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for the water brooks, So my soul longs for You, God.
My soul thirsts for the living God; When shall I come and be in God's presence?
My tears have been my sustenance day and night, While they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”
I recall these memories and pour out my soul within me. For I used to join the multitude and lead them to the house of God, With shouts of joy and thanksgiving, a throng celebrating a festival.
Why are you downcast, my soul? And why are you unsettled within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise Him For His saving presence, my God.
My soul is downcast within me; Therefore I remember You from the land of the Jordan And the heights of Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
The depths call to one another at the sound of Your waterfalls; All Your waves and breakers have passed over me.
The Lord will send His steadfast love in the daytime; And His song will accompany me in the night, A prayer to the God of my life.
I will say to God my rock, “Why have You forgotten me? Why must I go about mourning because of the enemy's oppression?”
As if crushing my bones, my adversaries taunt me, While they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”
Why are you downcast, my soul?

And why are you unsettled within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise Him For His saving presence, my God. Defend me, God, and argue my case against a nation without godliness; Rescue me from the deceitful and unjust person!
For You are the God of my refuge; why have You rejected me? Why do I go about mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
Send forth Your light and Your truth, they shall guide me; They shall bring me to Your sacred hill And to Your dwelling places.
Then I will approach the altar of God, To God, my greatest joy; And I will praise You with the lyre, God, my God.
Why are you downcast, my soul?

And why are you unsettled within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise Him For the help of His presence, my God. For the music director. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. God, we have heard with our ears, Our fathers have told us The work that You did in their days, In the days of old.
With Your mighty hand, You drove out the nations and established our ancestors; You afflicted the peoples and then set them free.
For by their own sword they did not possess the land, And their own arm did not save them, But Your right hand and Your arm and the light of Your presence, For You favoured them.
You are my sovereign, O God; Grant triumphs for Jacob.
Through You, we will repel our adversaries; In Your name, we will overcome those who rise against us.
For I will not rely on my bow, Nor will my sword deliver me.
But You have delivered us from our adversaries, And You have brought shame upon those who hate us.
In God, we have gloried all day long, And we will continually give thanks to Your name. Selah
Yet You have rejected us and brought us to dishonour, And do not go out with our armies.
You make us retreat from the enemy, And those who hate us have taken spoils for themselves.
You turn us over as sheep to be eaten, And have scattered us among the nations.
You have sold Your people for a pittance, And gained nothing from their sale.
You make us an object of reproach to our neighbours, Of scoffing and ridicule to those around us.
You have made us a proverb among the nations, A source of derision among the peoples.
All day long my dishonour is before me, And I am covered with my humiliation,
Because of the voice of one who taunts and reviles, Because of the enemy and the avenger.
All this has come upon us, yet we have not forgotten You, And we have remained true to Your covenant.
Our heart has not turned back, And our steps have not strayed from Your path,
Yet You have crushed us in a desolate place, And covered us with deep darkness.
If we had forgotten the name of our God Or reached out to a foreign god,
Would God not discover this? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
Yet for Your sake, we are in peril all day long; We are regarded as sheep destined for slaughter.
Awake, why do You sleep, Lord? Arise, do not reject us forever.
Why do You hide Your face And ignore our affliction and oppression?
For our souls have been brought down to the dust; Our bodies cling to the earth.
Arise, be our aid, And redeem us because of Your lovingkindness.

For the music director; according to the Shoshannim. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. A Song of Love. My heart is moved with a good theme; I address my verses to the King; My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
You are the fairest among the sons of men; Grace flows from Your lips; Therefore God has blessed You eternally.
Strap Your sword on Your thigh, Mighty One, In Your splendour and majesty!
And in Your majesty ride forth triumphantly, For the sake of truth, humility, and righteousness; Let Your right hand reveal marvellous things.
Your arrows are keen; The nations fall beneath You; Your arrows pierce the heart of the King’s foes.
Your throne, God, is forever and ever; The sceptre of Your kingdom is a sceptre of justice.
You cherish righteousness and detest wickedness; Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of joy above Your peers.
All Your garments are scented with myrrh, aloes, and cassia; From ivory palaces, stringed instruments have delighted You.
Daughters of kings are among Your noble women; At Your right hand stands the queen adorned in gold from Ophir.
Hear, daughter, observe and incline your ear: Leave behind your people and your father’s house;
Then the King will desire your beauty. Because He is your Lord, honour Him.
The daughter of Tyre will come with a gift; The wealthy among the people will seek your favour.
The King’s daughter is resplendent within; Her attire is woven with gold.
She will be brought to the King in colourful garments; The virgins, her companions who follow her, will be brought to You.
They will be brought with gladness and joy; They will enter the King’s palace.
In place of your fathers shall be your sons; You will appoint them as princes throughout the land.
I will cause Your name to be remembered in all generations; Therefore the peoples will praise You forever and ever.
For the music director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah, set to Alamoth. A Song. God is our refuge and strength, A very present help in times of trouble.
Thus, we will not fear, even if the earth trembles And the mountains fall into the depths of the sea;
Though its waters roar and foam, Though the mountains quake at its surging pride. Selah
There is a river whose streams bring joy to the city of God, The sacred dwelling places of the Most High.
God is within her, she will not be shaken; God will assist her at the break of day.
The nations were in turmoil, the kingdoms were shaken; He raised His voice, and the earth melted.
The Lord Almighty is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah
Come, behold the works of the Lord, Who has brought astounding events upon the earth.
He makes wars cease to the end of the earth; He breaks the bow and cuts the spear in two; He burns the chariots with fire.
“Be still and recognise that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted on the earth.”
The Lord Almighty is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold.
Selah For the music director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Clap your hands, all you peoples; Shout to God with a voice of joy.
For the Lord Most High is awe-inspiring, A great King over all the earth.
He brings peoples under our influence And nations beneath our feet.
He selects our heritage for us, The pride of Jacob whom He cherishes. Selah
God has ascended amidst joyful cries, The Lord, with the sound of a trumpet.
Offer praises to God, offer praises; Offer praises to our King, offer praises.
For God is the King of all the earth; Offer praises with a psalm of insight.
God rules over the nations, God is seated on His holy throne.
The leaders of the peoples have gathered As the people of the God of Abraham, For the shields of the earth belong to God; He is highly exalted.
A Song; a Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised In the city of our God, His holy mountain.
Majestic in its height, bringing joy to the whole earth, Is Mount Zion on the northern side, The city of the great King.
God has revealed Himself as a refuge Within her palaces.
Indeed, the kings gathered, They moved on together.
They observed it and were astounded; They were alarmed, they fled swiftly.
Panic seized them there, Anguish, like a woman in childbirth.
With the east wind You shatter the ships of Tarshish.
Just as we have heard, so we have seen In the city of the Lord of armies, in the city of our God; God will establish her forever. Selah
We have reflected on Your mercy, God, In the midst of Your temple.
As Your name is renowned, God, So is Your praise throughout the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
Let Mount Zion be joyful, Let the daughters of Judah celebrate Because of Your judgments.
Walk around Zion and go around her; Count her towers;
Consider her ramparts; Pass through her palaces, So that you may tell of it to the next generation.
For this God is our God forever and ever; He will guide us even unto death.
For the music director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Hear this, all peoples; Listen, all inhabitants of the world,
Whether of humble or noble birth, The wealthy and the poor together.
My mouth will speak with wisdom, And the thoughts of my heart will be insightful.
I will listen to a proverb; I will reveal my riddle with the harp.
Why should I fear in days of adversity, When the guilt of my foes surrounds me,
Those who rely on their wealth And take pride in their abundant riches?
No one can ever redeem another, Or offer God a ransom for them—
For the redemption of their soul is beyond price, And they should cease imagining it forever—
So that they might live on eternally, That they might not undergo decay.
For he observes that even the wise meet their end; The foolish and the senseless perish alike And leave their wealth to others.
They believe their houses will last forever And their dwelling places for all generations; They have named their lands after themselves.
But man in his splendour will not endure; He is like the animals that perish.
Such is the path of those who are foolish, And of those who follow them and approve their sayings. Selah

Like sheep they are destined for Sheol; Death will shepherd them; And the upright will have dominion over them in the morning, And their form shall be consumed by Sheol So that they have no exalted home.
But God will redeem my soul from the grasp of Sheol, For He will take me to Himself. Selah
Do not be afraid when someone becomes wealthy, When the splendour of their house increases;
For when they die, they will take nothing with them; Their wealth will not follow them down.
Though in life they congratulate themselves— And though others praise you when you prosper—
They will join the company of their ancestors; They will never again see the light.
Mankind, in its splendour, yet without understanding, Is like the animals that perish.
A Psalm of Asaph. The Mighty One, God, the Lord, has spoken, And summoned the earth, from the rising of the sun to its setting.
From Zion, the epitome of beauty, God has revealed His light.
May our God come and not remain silent; A consuming fire precedes Him, And a mighty storm swirls around Him.
He calls upon the heavens above, And the earth, to judge His people:
“Gather to Me My faithful ones, Those who have entered into a covenant with Me through sacrifice.”
And the heavens proclaim His righteousness, For God Himself is the judge. Selah
“Listen, My people, and I will speak; Israel, I will bear witness against you; I am God, your God.
I do not reprimand you for your sacrifices, And your burnt offerings are ever before Me.
I will not take a bull from your house, Nor male goats from your pens.
For every creature of the forest is Mine, The cattle on a thousand hills.
I am familiar with every bird of the mountains, And all that moves in the field belongs to Me.
If I were hungry, I would not inform you, For the world is Mine, and all it holds.
Do I consume the flesh of bulls Or drink the blood of male goats?
Present to God an offering of gratitude And fulfil your promises to the Most High;
Call upon Me when you face adversity; I will rescue you, and you will honour Me.”
But to the wicked God declares, “What right have you to recite My statutes And to speak of My covenant?
For you despise correction, And you cast My words aside.
When you witness a thief, you align with him, And you keep company with adulterers.
You freely speak evil, And your tongue weaves deceit.
You sit and speak against your brother; You malign your own mother's son.
These actions you have taken, and I remained silent; You assumed I was like you; I will correct you and lay out the case before your eyes.
Now reflect on this, you who forget God, Lest I tear you apart, with none to rescue you.
Whoever offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving honours Me; And to the one who conducts his way rightly, I will reveal the salvation of God.”

For the music director. A Psalm of David, when Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Be gracious to me, God, according to Your mercy; According to the greatness of Your compassion, blot out my wrongdoings.
Cleanse me thoroughly from my guilt And purify me from my sin.
For I am aware of my wrongdoings, And my sin is ever before me.
Against You, and You alone, I have sinned And done what is evil in Your sight, So that You are justified in Your words And blameless in Your judgement.
Indeed, I was born into a state of guilt, And in sin my mother conceived me.
Truly, You desire truth deep within, And in the hidden part You will make wisdom known to me.
Cleanse me with hyssop, and I will be clean; Purify me, and I will be whiter than snow.
Let me hear joy and gladness, Let the bones You have humbled rejoice.
Hide Your face from my sins And blot out all my guilty deeds.
Create in me a pure heart, God, And renew a steadfast spirit within me.
Do not banish me from Your presence, And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
Return to me the joy of Your salvation, And sustain me with a willing spirit.
Then I will teach wrongdoers Your ways, And sinners will return to You.
Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, God, the God of my salvation; Then my tongue will joyfully sing of Your righteousness.
Lord, open my lips, So that my mouth may proclaim Your praise.
For You do not delight in sacrifice, or I would bring it; You do not take pleasure in burnt offerings.
The offerings of God are a humble spirit; A heart that is broken and contrite, God, You will not despise.
By Your favour do good to Zion; Build the walls of Jerusalem.
Then You will take pleasure in righteous sacrifices, In burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings; Then bulls will be offered on Your altar.

For the music director. A Maskil of David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, and said to him, “David has come to the house of Ahimelech.” Why do you boast in evil, you mighty man? The faithfulness of God endures all day long.
Your tongue plots destruction, Like a sharp razor, you who practice deceit.
You prefer evil over good, Falsehood over speaking what is right. Selah
You delight in words that consume, You deceitful tongue.
Yet God will bring you down permanently; He will seize you and remove you from your dwelling, And uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
The righteous will observe and be in awe, And they will mock him, saying,
“Behold, the man who would not make God his refuge, But trusted in the abundance of his riches, And sought refuge in his own destruction.”
But as for me, I am like a flourishing olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the faithfulness of God forever and ever.
I will praise You forever, because You have accomplished it, And I will wait on Your name, for it is good, in the presence of Your faithful ones.

For the music director; according to Mahalath. A Maskil of David. The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, and have committed abominable injustice; There is no one who does good.
God observed from heaven upon humanity To see if there is anyone who possesses understanding, Who seeks after God.
All have turned aside; together they have become corrupt; There is no one who does good, not even one.
Do the workers of injustice lack knowledge, Who consume My people as they would bread, And have not called upon God?
They were in great fear where there was no cause for fear; For God scattered the bones of those who encamped against you; You put them to shame, because God had rejected them.
Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion!

When God restores the fortunes of His people, Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad. For the music director; on stringed instruments. A Maskil of David, when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, “Is David not keeping himself hidden among us?” Save me, God, by Your name, And vindicate me by Your power.
Attend to my prayer, God; Give ear to the words I speak.
For outsiders have risen against me, And ruthless men have sought my life; They have not placed God before them. Selah
Indeed, God is my helper; The Lord is the upholder of my soul.
He will repay the evil to my enemies; In Your faithfulness, bring them down.
Freely I will offer sacrifices to You; I will extol Your name, Lord, for it is good.
For He has delivered me from all distress, And my eye has gazed with satisfaction upon my foes.
For the music director; on stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. Listen to my prayer, God; And do not hide Yourself from my pleading.
Turn Your ear to me and respond to me; I am unsettled in my complaint and greatly troubled,
Due to the voice of the adversary, Due to the oppression of the wicked; For they heap trouble upon me And in anger they bear a grudge against me.
My heart is deeply pained within me, And the terrors of death have seized me.
Fear and trembling have beset me, And horror has engulfed me.
I said, “Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and find rest.
Behold, I would escape far away, I would spend my night in the wilderness. Selah
I would hasten to my place of refuge From the stormy wind and fierce gale.”
Confound them, Lord, divide their tongues, For I have witnessed violence and strife in the city.
Day and night they encircle her upon her walls, And wrongdoing and harm are within her.
Ruin is within her; Oppression and deceit do not leave her streets.
For it is not an enemy who mocks me, then I could bear it; Nor is it one who despises me who has risen against me, Then I could hide myself from him.
But it is you, a person my equal, My companion and my confidant;
We who shared sweet fellowship together, Walked in the house of God among the throng.
May death come upon them unexpectedly; May they descend alive to Sheol, For wickedness is in their dwelling, among them.
As for me, I will call upon God, And the Lord will deliver me.
Evening and morning and at noon, I will lament and groan, And He will hear my voice.
He will rescue my soul in peace from the battle against me, For many are those who oppose me.
God will hear and humble them— Even the one who sits enthroned from ancient times— Selah Because they do not change, And do not fear God.
He has stretched out his hands against those who were at peace with him; He has broken his covenant.
