His words were smoother than butter, But his heart was set on war; His words were softer than oil, Yet they were drawn swords.
Cast your burden upon the Lord and He will uphold you; He will never permit the righteous to be moved.
But You, God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction; Men of bloodshed and deceit will not live out half their days.

But I will place my trust in You. For the music director; according to Jonath elem rehokim. A Mikhtam of David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be gracious to me, God, for a man has trampled upon me; Fighting all day long he oppresses me.
My adversaries have pursued me throughout the day, For numerous are those who contend against me with pride.
In times of fear, I will place my trust in You.
In God, whose word I praise, In God I have placed my trust; I shall not be afraid. What can mere mortals do to me?
Throughout the day they twist my words; All their schemes are against me for harm.
They attack, they lurk, They watch my steps, Just as they have waited to take my life.
Because of their wickedness, is there a way of escape for them? In Your anger, bring the nations down, God!
You have noted my sufferings; Collect my tears in Your bottle. Are they not recorded in Your book?
Then my foes will retreat on the day I call; This I know, that God is with me.
In God, whose word I praise, In the Lord, whose message I honour,
In God I have placed my trust, I shall not be afraid. What can the world do to me?
Your promises are binding upon me, God; I will offer thanksgiving sacrifices to You.
For You have rescued my soul from death, Indeed my feet from stumbling, So that I may walk before God In the brightness of life.

For the music director; set to Al- tashheth. A Mikhtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be gracious to me, God, be gracious to me, For my soul takes refuge in You; And in the shadow of Your wings I will take refuge Until destruction passes by.
I will call out to God Most High, To God who fulfils His purpose for me.
He will send from heaven and save me; He rebukes the one who tramples upon me. Selah God will send His favour and His truth.
My soul is among lions; I must lie down among those who devour, Among sons of mankind, whose teeth are spears and arrows, And their tongue is a sharp sword.
Be lifted high above the heavens, God; May Your glory be over all the earth.
They have laid a snare for my steps; My soul is bowed down; They dug a pit before me; They themselves have fallen into it. Selah
My heart is unwavering, God, my heart is unwavering; I will sing, indeed, I will sing praises!
Arise, my soul! Arise, harp and lyre! I will awaken the morning light.
I will give thanks to You, Lord, among the peoples; I will sing praises to You among the nations.
For Your mercy reaches to the heavens, And Your truth to the clouds.
Be lifted high above the heavens, God; May Your glory be over all the earth.
For the music director; set to Al- tashheth. A Mikhtam of David. Do you indeed speak righteousness, you gods? Do you judge fairly, you sons of mankind?
Yet, in heart you engage in wrongdoing; On earth you pave a path for the violence of your hands.
The wicked have strayed from the very beginning; Those who speak falsehoods wander from birth.
They possess venom akin to that of a serpent; Like a deaf cobra that closes its ear,
So that it does not heed the voice of charmers, Or a skilful enchanter.
God, break their teeth within their mouth; Remove the fangs of the young lions, Lord.
May they vanish like water that flows away; When he bends his bow, may his arrows be as headless shafts.
May they be like a snail that dissolves into slime, Like a miscarriage that never sees the sun.
Before your pots can feel the heat of thorns, He will sweep them away with a whirlwind, both the fresh and the burning.
The righteous will rejoice when he witnesses justice; He will bathe his feet in the downfall of the wicked.
And people will declare, “Truly there is a reward for the righteous; Truly there is a God who judges on the earth!”

For the music director; set to Al- tashheth. A Mikhtam of David, when Saul sent men and they watched the house to kill him. Rescue me from my enemies, my God; Set me securely on high away from those who rise up against me.
Deliver me from those who commit injustice, And save me from violent men.
For behold, they have laid a trap for my life; Fierce men attack me, Not because of any wrongdoing or sin of mine, Lord,
Without any guilt on my part, they run and take their stand against me. Rouse Yourself to help me, and see!
You, Lord God of hosts, the God of Israel, Awake to bring justice to all the nations; Show no favour to those who act treacherously in wrongdoing. Selah
They return at evening, they growl like a dog, And roam around the city.
Behold, they spew out with their mouths; Swords are in their lips, For, they say, “Who hears?”
Yet You, Lord, find them laughable; You hold all the nations in derision.
In His strength, I will keep watch for You, For God is my refuge.
My God in His faithfulness will come to me; God will let me look in triumph upon my enemies.
Do not destroy them, lest my people forget; Scatter them by Your power and bring them down, Lord, our shield.
Because of the sin of their mouths and the words of their lips, May they be caught in their pride, And because of curses and lies which they speak.
Consume them in wrath, consume them so they will be no more; So that people may know that God rules in Jacob, To the ends of the earth. Selah
They return at evening, they growl like a dog, And roam around the city.
They wander about for food, And grumble if they are not satisfied.
But as for me, I will sing of Your strength; Yes, I will joyfully sing of Your mercy in the morning, For You have been my refuge And a place of shelter on the day of my distress.
My strength, I will sing praises to You; For God is my refuge, the God who shows me mercy.

For the music director; according to Shushan Eduth. A Mikhtam of David, to teach; when he fought with Aram-naharaim and with Aram-zobah, and Joab returned and struck twelve thousand of Edom in the Valley of Salt. God, You have rejected us. You have broken us; You have been angry; restore us.
You have made the land quake, You have split it open; Heal its cracks, for it totters.
You have caused Your people to endure hardship; You have given us wine that causes us to stagger.
You have given a banner to those who fear You, So that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah
So that Your beloved may be rescued, Save with Your right hand, and answer us!
God has spoken in His holiness: “I will triumph, I will divide Shechem, And measure out the Valley of Succoth.
Gilead is Mine, and Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim also is the helmet of My head; Judah is My sceptre.
Moab is My basin for washing; Over Edom I will cast My sandal; Philistia, shout aloud because of Me!”
Who will guide me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Have You Yourself not rejected us, God? And will You not go forth with our armies, God?
Give us help against the enemy, For rescue by man is worthless.
Through God we will achieve victory, And it is He who will overcome our foes.
For the music director; on a stringed instrument. A Psalm of David. Hear my cry, God; Give Your attention to my prayer.
From the farthest reaches, I call to You when my heart is weary; Guide me to the rock that is higher than I.
For You have been my refuge, A strong tower against the adversary.
May I reside in Your tent forever; Let me find safety in the shelter of Your wings. Selah
For You have listened to my vows, God; You have granted me the heritage of those who revere Your name.
You will extend the king’s life; His years will span generations.
He will abide before God forever; Appoint mercy and truth, that they may guard him.
So I will sing praise to Your name forever, So that I may fulfil my vows day by day.
For the music director; according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. My soul waits in silence for God alone; From Him comes my salvation.
He alone is my rock and my salvation, My fortress; I will not be greatly moved.
How long will you assail a man, That you may bring him down, all of you, Like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence?
They have devised only to topple him from his high position; They take pleasure in falsehood; They bless with their mouth, But inwardly they curse. Selah
My soul, remain silent before God alone, For my hope comes from Him.
He alone is my rock and my salvation, My refuge; I will not be moved.
My salvation and my honour depend on God; The rock of my strength, my refuge is in God.
Trust in Him at all times, you people; Pour out your hearts before Him; God is a shelter for us. Selah
People of low standing are but a breath, and those of rank are a deception; In the balances they rise. Together they are lighter than air.
Do not rely on oppression, And do not place vain hope in robbery; If wealth increases, do not fix your heart on it.
God has spoken once; Twice I have heard this: That strength belongs to God;
And steadfast love is Yours, Lord, For You repay a person according to their deeds.

A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah. God, You are my God; I shall be watching for You; My soul thirsts for You, my flesh yearns for You, In a dry and exhausted land, where there is no water.
Thus have I beheld You in the sanctuary, To witness Your power and glory.
Because Your favour is better than life, My lips will praise You.
Therefore, I will bless You throughout my life; I will raise my hands in Your name.
My soul is fulfilled as with rich food, And my mouth offers praises with joyful lips.
As I think of You upon my bed, I reflect on You during the night watches,
For You have been my support, And under the shelter of Your wings, I sing for joy.
My soul holds fast to You; Your right hand upholds me.
Yet those who seek to harm my life Will descend into the depths of the earth.
They will be delivered to the power of the sword; They will become prey for foxes.
However, the king will find joy in God; Everyone who swears by Him will exult, For the mouths of those who speak falsehoods will be silenced.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, God, in my complaint; Protect my life from dread of the enemy.
Conceal me from the secret schemes of evildoers, From the turmoil of those who commit injustice,
Who have sharpened their tongues like a sword. They directed harsh words as their arrows,
To strike unexpectedly at the innocent; Suddenly they attack without fear.
They strengthen their wicked plans; They discuss laying traps in secret; They say, “Who can perceive them?”
They plot injustices, saying, “We are prepared with a clever scheme”; For the inner thoughts and the heart of a person are profound.
But God will send an arrow into them; Suddenly they will be struck.
So they will be made to stumble; Their own tongues will turn against them; All who see them will shake their heads.
Then all people will be in awe, And they will proclaim the deeds of God, And will ponder what He has accomplished.
The righteous will rejoice in the Lord and seek shelter in Him; And all the upright in heart will exult.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. A Song. There will be silence before You, and praise in Zion, God, And the vow will be fulfilled to You.
You who listen to prayer, To You all people come.
Wrongdoings prevail against me; As for our offences, You forgive them.
Blessed is the one You choose and bring near to You; He will dwell in Your courtyards. We will be satisfied with the goodness of Your house, Your sacred temple.
By wondrous deeds You answer us with righteousness, God of our salvation, You who are the hope of all the ends of the earth and the distant seas;
Who establishes the mountains by His strength, Who is surrounded with might;
Who calms the roaring of the seas, The roaring of their waves, And the unrest of the nations.
Those who dwell at the ends of the earth are in awe of Your signs; You make the sunrise and the sunset shout for joy.
You visit the earth and make it overflow; You greatly enrich it; The stream of God is full of water; You prepare their grain, for thus You prepare the earth.
You water its furrows abundantly, You level its ridges, You soften it with showers, You bless its growth.
You have crowned the year with Your goodness, And Your paths overflow with abundance.
The pastures of the wilderness overflow, And the hills wrap themselves with joy.
The meadows are adorned with flocks, And the valleys are covered with grain; They shout for joy, yes, they sing.
For the music director. A Song. A Psalm. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth;
Sing of the splendour of His name; Make His praise resound gloriously.
Say to God, “How marvellous are Your works! Because of the greatness of Your power, Your enemies will feign obedience to You.
All the earth will worship You, And will sing praises to You; They will celebrate Your name.” Selah
Come and witness the works of God, Who is wondrous in His deeds toward the children of mankind.
He turned the sea into dry land; They passed through the river on foot; Let us rejoice there, in Him!
He rules by His might forever; His eyes observe the nations; The rebellious shall not exalt themselves. Selah
Praise our God, you peoples, And let His praise be heard far and wide,
Who preserves our lives, And does not let our feet stumble.
For You have tested us, God; You have refined us as silver is refined.
You brought us into the net; You placed a heavy burden upon us.
You allowed men to ride over our heads; We went through fire and water. Yet You brought us out to a place of abundance.
I shall come into Your house with burnt offerings; I shall fulfil my vows to You,
Which my lips have spoken And my mouth declared when I was in distress.
I shall offer to You burnt offerings of well-fed animals, With the smoke of rams; I shall present an offering of bulls with male goats. Selah
Come and listen, all who fear God, And I will recount what He has done for my soul.
I cried out to Him with my mouth, And His praise was on my tongue.
If I cherish wickedness in my heart, The Lord will not listen;
But God has listened; He has attended to the voice of my prayer.
Blessed be God, Who has not turned away my prayer, Nor withheld His favour from me.
For the music director; with stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song. God be gracious to us and bless us, And cause His face to shine upon us— Selah
So that Your ways may be recognised across the earth, Your deliverance among all nations.
Let the peoples give You praise, God; Let all the peoples give You praise.
Let the nations rejoice and sing with gladness; For You will judge the peoples with equity And lead the nations on the earth. Selah
Let the peoples give You praise, God; Let all the peoples give You praise.
The earth has brought forth its harvest; God, our God, bestows His blessings upon us.
God bestows His blessings upon us, So that all the ends of the earth may hold Him in reverence.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. A Song. God arises, His enemies scatter, And those who hate Him flee from His presence.
As smoke is blown away, so You blow them away; As wax melts before the fire, So the wicked perish before God.
But the righteous are glad; they rejoice before God, Yes, they rejoice with joy.
Sing to God, offer praises to His name; Lift up the One who journeys through the deserts, Whose name is the Lord, and be joyful before Him.
A protector of orphans and a defender of widows, Is God in His holy dwelling.
God provides a home for those who are alone; He leads out the prisoners into prosperity, But the rebellious dwell in a dry land.
God, when You went out before Your people, When You marched through the desert, Selah
The earth trembled; The heavens also poured rain at the presence of God; Sinai itself shook at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
You sent abundant rain, God; You restored Your inheritance when it was weary.
Your people settled in it; In Your goodness You provided for the needy, God.
The Lord delivers the message; The women who proclaim the tidings are a great host:
“Kings of armies retreat, they retreat, And she who stays at home divides the plunder!”
When you rest among the sheepfolds, You are like the wings of a dove covered with silver, And its feathers with shimmering gold.
When the Almighty scattered kings there, It was as if it snowed on Zalmon.
The mountain of Bashan is a majestic mountain; The mountain of Bashan is a mountain of many peaks.
Why do you gaze with envy, you rugged mountains, At the mountain God has chosen for His dwelling? Indeed, the Lord will reside there forever.
The chariots of God are countless, thousands upon thousands; The Lord is among them as He was at Sinai, in holiness.
You have ascended on high, You have led captives in Your train; You have received gifts from people, Even from the rebellious, That the Lord God may dwell there.
Praise be to the Lord, who daily carries our burdens, The God who is our salvation. Selah
Our God is a God who saves; To the Sovereign Lord belong ways of escape from death.
God will surely crush the heads of His enemies, The hairy crown of those who persist in their sins.
The Lord declared, “I will bring them back from Bashan. I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
So that your foot may wade in their blood, And the tongues of your dogs may have their share from the foes.”
They have witnessed Your procession, God, The procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
The singers lead the way, the musicians follow after, Among the young women playing tambourines.
Bless God in the assemblies, The Lord, you who are from the fountain of Israel.
Benjamin, the youngest, is there, leading them, The princes of Judah in their throng, The princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
Your God has ordained your strength; Display Your power, God, You who have acted on our behalf.
Because of Your temple in Jerusalem, Rulers will bring tribute to You.
Rebuke the beasts among the reeds, The herd of bulls with the calves of the nations, Trampling down the pieces of silver; He has scattered the nations who delight in conflict.
Envoys will come from Egypt; Cush will quickly stretch out her hands to God.
Sing to God, all you kingdoms of the earth, Sing praises to the Lord, Selah
To Him who rides upon the highest heavens, which are from ancient times; Behold, He speaks with His voice, a mighty voice.
Ascribe power to God; His splendour is over Israel, And His strength is in the skies.
God, You are awe-inspiring from Your sanctuary.

The God of Israel Himself gives strength and power to the people. Blessed be God! For the music director; according to Shoshannim. A Psalm of David. Save me, God, For the waters have threatened my life.
I have sunk into deep mire, and there is no foothold; I have come into deep waters, and the flood overwhelms me.
I am exhausted from crying; my throat is dry; My eyes fail as I wait for my God.
Those who hate me without reason are more than the hairs of my head; Those who seek to destroy me are powerful, those who oppose me with falsehoods; What I did not steal, I must now restore.
God, You are aware of my foolishness, And my guilt is not concealed from You.
May those who wait for You not be put to shame because of me, Lord God of armies; May those who seek You not be dishonoured because of me, God of Israel,
Because for Your sake I have endured disgrace; Dishonour has covered my face.
I have become alienated from my brothers, And a stranger to my mother’s sons.
For zeal for Your house has consumed me, And the insults of those who insult You have fallen on me.
When I wept and fasted, It brought me reproach.
When I wore sackcloth as my garment, I became a byword to them.
Those who sit at the gate talk about me, And I am the subject of songs by those who are intoxicated.
But as for me, my prayer is to You, Lord, at a favourable time; God, in the abundance of Your mercy, Answer me with Your saving truth.
Deliver me from the mire and do not let me sink; May I be rescued from those who hate me, and from the depths of the water.
May the floodwaters not sweep over me Nor the deep engulf me, Nor the pit close its mouth over me.
Respond to me, Lord, for Your kindness is abundant; In Your great compassion, turn towards me,
And do not hide Your face from Your servant, For I am in distress; respond to me quickly.
Draw near to my soul and redeem it; Rescue me because of my enemies!
You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonour; All my adversaries are known to You.
Reproach has broken my heart, and I am so weak. And I looked for sympathy, but there was none, And for comforters, but I found none.
They also gave me bitter food, And for my thirst they offered me sour wine.
May their table before them become a snare; And when they feel secure, may it become a trap.
May their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, And make their bodies tremble continually.
Pour out Your indignation on them, And may Your fierce anger overtake them.
May their camp be deserted; May there be none dwelling in their tents.
For they have persecuted the one You have struck, And they recount the pain of those You have wounded.
Add iniquity to their iniquity, And may they not enter into Your righteousness.
May they be blotted out of the book of life, And may they not be listed with the righteous.
But I am afflicted and in pain; May Your salvation, God, set me securely on high.
I will honour the name of God with a song, And exalt Him with thanksgiving.
And it will please the Lord more than an ox Or a bull with horns and hoofs.
The humble have seen it and are glad; You who seek God, let your hearts be revived.
For the Lord hears the needy, And does not despise His prisoners.
Let the heavens and the earth extol Him, The seas and all that move within them.
For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah, So that they may dwell there and possess it.
The offspring of His servants will inherit it, And those who love His name will live in it.
For the music director. A Psalm of David; to bring to remembrance. God, hurry to save me; Lord, hurry to help me!
May those who seek my life be put to shame and humiliated; May those who delight in my harm be turned back and dishonoured.
May those who mock with “Aha, aha!” be turned back Due to their disgrace.
May all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; And may those who love Your salvation continually say, “May God be exalted!”
But I am afflicted and needy; Hasten to me, God!
You are my help and my saviour; Lord, do not delay. In You, Lord, I seek shelter; May I never be disgraced.
In Your righteousness deliver me and save me; Incline Your ear to me and assist me.
Be to me a rock of dwelling to which I may continually come; You have given the command to save me, For You are my rock and my fortress.
Rescue me, my God, from the hand of the wicked, From the grasp of the wrongdoer and the ruthless.
For You are my hope; Lord God, You have been my trust since my youth.
I have relied on You since my birth; You are the one who brought me forth from my mother’s womb; My praise is always of You.
I have become a wonder to many, For You are my strong refuge.
My mouth is filled with Your praise And with Your glory throughout the day.
Do not cast me away in the time of my old age; Do not abandon me when my strength fails.
For my enemies have spoken against me; And those who seek my life have conspired together,
Saying, “God has forsaken him; Pursue and capture him, for there is no one to deliver him.”
God, do not be distant from me; My God, hasten to assist me!
May those who are adversaries of my soul be put to shame and consumed; May they be covered with disgrace and dishonour, those who seek to harm me.
But as for me, I will hope continually, And will praise You more and more.
My mouth will recount Your righteousness And Your salvation all day long, For I do not know the art of writing.
I will come with the mighty deeds of the Lord God; I will speak of Your righteousness, Yours alone.
God, You have instructed me from my youth, And I still proclaim Your wondrous deeds.
And even when I am old and grey, God, do not leave me, Until I declare Your strength to this generation, Your power to all who are to come.
For Your righteousness, God, extends to the skies, You who have accomplished marvellous deeds; God, who can compare to You?
You who have allowed me to face many troubles and hardships Will restore me once more, And will lift me again from the depths of despair.
May You enhance my honour And turn to bring me solace.
I will also praise You with a harp, And proclaim Your faithfulness, my God; I will sing praises to You with the lyre, Holy One of Israel.
My lips will shout for joy when I sing praises to You; And my soul, which You have rescued.
My tongue also will speak of Your righteousness all day long; For they are put to shame, for they are humiliated who seek my harm.
A Psalm of Solomon. Give the king Your judgments, God, And Your righteousness to the king’s son.
May he govern Your people with fairness And Your afflicted ones with equity.
May the mountains yield peace to the people, And the hills, through righteousness.
May he defend the afflicted among the people, Save the children of the needy, And subdue the oppressor.
May they revere You as long as the sun endures, And as long as the moon endures, throughout all generations.
May he descend like rain upon the mown grass, Like showers that nourish the earth.
May the righteous thrive in his days, And may there be an abundance of peace until the moon is no more.
May he also have dominion from sea to sea, And from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth.
May the nomads of the desert bow before him, And may his enemies lick the dust.
May the kings of Tarshish and of the islands bring offerings; May the kings of Sheba and Seba present tributes.
And may all kings bow down before him, May all nations serve him.
For he will deliver the needy when they call for help, The afflicted also, and those who have no helper.
He will show compassion to the poor and needy, And he will preserve the lives of the needy.
He will redeem their life from oppression and violence, And their blood will be valued in his sight.
So may he live, and may the gold of Sheba be given to him; And may they pray for him continually; May they bless him all day long.

May there be an abundance of grain on the earth, even on the tops of the mountains; Its fruit will wave like the cedars of Lebanon; And may those from the city flourish like the vegetation of the earth.
May his name endure forever; May his name continue as long as the sun shines; And may people bless themselves through him; May all nations call him blessed.
Praised be the Lord God, the God of Israel, Who alone performs wonders.
And praised be His glorious name forever; And may the whole earth be filled with His glory. Amen and Amen.
The prayers of David the son of Jesse are concluded.
A Psalm of Asaph. God certainly is good to Israel, To those who are pure in heart!
Yet my feet nearly stumbled, My steps almost slipped.
For I was envious of the arrogant When I observed the success of the wicked.
For they seem to have no struggles in their death, And their bodies are well-nourished.
They do not face the troubles of others, Nor are they plagued like the rest of humanity.
Therefore arrogance adorns them like a necklace; Violence clothes them like a garment.
Their eyes bulge with abundance; The imaginations of their heart overflow.
They scoff and speak with malice; They speak with haughty intent.
They set their mouths against the heavens, And their tongues strut through the earth.
Therefore their people turn to them, And they drink up waters in abundance.
They say, “How can God know? Is there knowledge with the Most High?”
Behold, these are the wicked; Always at ease, they increase in wealth.
Surely I have kept my heart pure in vain And washed my hands in innocence;
For I have been afflicted all day long, And chastened every morning.
If I had resolved to speak like this, Behold, I would have been unfaithful to Your children.
When I tried to understand this, It was a burden to me
Until I entered the sanctuary of God; Then I understood their final destiny.
Surely You place them on slippery paths; You cast them down to ruin.
How suddenly they are destroyed, Completely swept away by terrors!
Like a dream when one awakens, Lord, When You arise, You will despise their form.
When my heart was grieved And my spirit was embittered,
I was senseless and ignorant; I was like a brute beast before You.
Nevertheless, I am continually with You; You uphold me by my right hand.
You lead me with Your wisdom, And afterward You will receive me into honour.
Whom have I in heaven but You? And on earth, I desire nothing besides You.
My flesh and my heart may falter, But God remains the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
For indeed, those who are far from You will perish; You destroy all who are unfaithful to You.
But as for me, it is good to be near God; I have made the Sovereign Lord my refuge, That I may tell of all Your deeds.
A Maskil of Asaph. God, why have You rejected us forever? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
Recall Your people, whom You acquired long ago, Whom You have reclaimed as the tribe of Your inheritance; And this Mount Zion, where You have resided.
Approach the desolate ruins; The enemy has laid waste to everything in the sanctuary.
Your enemies roar in the heart of Your meeting place; They have set up their own emblems as symbols.
It seems like one bringing up An axe into a forest of trees.
And now they break down all its carved work With hatchet and hammers.
They have reduced Your sanctuary to ashes; They have desecrated the dwelling place of Your name.
They said in their heart, “Let’s completely subdue them.” They have set fire to all the meeting places of God in the land.
We do not see our signs; There is no longer any prophet, Nor is there anyone among us who knows the duration.
How long, God, will the enemy taunt You? Shall the enemy treat Your name with disrespect forever?
Why do You hold back Your hand, even Your right hand? Release it from Your bosom and bring justice!
Yet God is my King from long ago, Who performs deeds of salvation in the midst of the earth.
You parted the sea by Your strength; You shattered the heads of the sea creatures in the waters.
You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You provided him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
You opened springs and torrents; You dried up perpetual streams.
Yours is the day, Yours also is the night; You have set in place the light and the sun.
You have defined all the boundaries of the earth; You have fashioned summer and winter.
Remember this, Lord, that the enemy has taunted You, And a foolish people has treated Your name with disrespect.
Do not surrender the soul of Your turtledove to the wild beast; Do not forget the life of Your afflicted forever.
Consider the covenant; For the dark places of the land are full of the haunts of violence.
May the oppressed not return dishonoured; May the afflicted and the needy praise Your name.
Rise, God, and defend Your own cause; Remember how the foolish person taunts You all day long.
Do not forget the voice of Your enemies, The uproar of those who rise against You, which ascends continually.
For the music director; set to Al- tashheth. A Psalm of Asaph, a Song. We give thanks to You, God, we give thanks, For Your name is near; People declare Your wondrous works.
“When I choose the right moment, It is I who judge with fairness.
The earth and all who dwell in it shake; It is I who have firmly set its pillars. Selah
I said to the boastful, ‘Refrain from boasting,’ And to the wicked, ‘Do not raise your horn;
Do not raise your horn on high, Do not speak with arrogant pride.’”
For exaltation does not come from the east, nor from the west, Nor from the desert;
But God is the Judge; He brings down one and raises up another.
For a cup is in the hand of the Lord, and the wine foams; It is well mixed, and He pours out of it; Certainly all the wicked of the earth must drain and drink its dregs.
Yet I will proclaim it forever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
And He will cut off all the horns of the wicked, But the horns of the righteous will be exalted.
For the music director; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph, a Song. God is revered in Judah; His name holds great renown in Israel.
His sanctuary is in Salem; His abode is also in Zion.
There He shattered the fiery arrows, The shield, the sword, and the implements of war. Selah
You are resplendent, More majestic than the mountains full of prey.
The courageous were despoiled, They fell into slumber; And none of the warriors could wield his hands.
At Your rebuke, God of Jacob, Both rider and horse were plunged into a deep sleep.
You, You indeed are to be revered, And who may stand in Your presence, when You are provoked?
You made judgment resound from heaven; The earth trembled and was still,
When God rose to execute judgment, To deliver all the humble of the earth. Selah
For the fury of mankind shall extol You; You will gird Yourself with the remainder of wrath.
Make vows to the Lord your God and fulfill them; All who are near Him are to present gifts to Him who is to be revered.
He will humble the spirit of princes; He is revered by the kings of the earth.
For the music director; according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph. My voice rises to God, and I will cry aloud; My voice rises to God, and He will hear me.
In my distress, I sought the Lord; In the night, my hand was extended without tiring; My soul found no comfort.
When I think of God, I become unsettled; When I sigh, my spirit grows faint. Selah
You have kept my eyes from closing; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
I have reflected on the days of old, The years of times past.
I will recall my song in the night; I will meditate in my heart, And my spirit reflects:
Will the Lord reject forever? And will He never show favour again?
Has His favour ceased forever? Has His promise come to an end for all time?
Has God forgotten to be gracious, Or has He in anger withheld His compassion? Selah
Then I said, “This is my sorrow, That the right hand of the Most High has altered.”
I will recall the deeds of the Lord; Surely I will remember Your marvellous works of old.
I will reflect on all Your works, And on Your deeds with gratitude.
Your way, God, is sacred; What deity is great like our God?
You are the God who performs wonders; You have revealed Your strength among the nations.
By Your power, You have redeemed Your people, The descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
The waters saw You, God; The waters saw You and were in anguish; The ocean depths also trembled.
The clouds poured out water; The skies resounded; Your arrows flashed to and fro.
The sound of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; The lightning illuminated the world; The earth trembled and shook.
Your path was through the sea, And Your way through the mighty waters, And Your footprints were unseen.
You guided Your people like a flock By the hand of Moses and Aaron.
A Maskil of Asaph. Listen, my people, to my instruction; Incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
I will speak in parables; I will share ancient riddles,
Which we have heard and known, And which our ancestors have shared with us.
We will not hide them from their children, But we will declare to the next generation the praises of the Lord, And His power and the marvellous works He has accomplished.
For He established a testimony in Jacob, And set a law in Israel, Which He instructed our ancestors So they could teach to their children,
So that the next generation would know, the children yet to be born, That they would rise and tell them to their children,
So that they would place their trust in God And not forget the deeds of God, But adhere to His commandments,
And not be like their ancestors, A stubborn and rebellious generation, A generation that did not prepare its heart, And whose spirit was not faithful to God.
The sons of Ephraim, armed with bows, Yet retreated on the day of battle.
They did not uphold the covenant of God And refused to follow His law;
They forgot His deeds And the miracles He had shown them.
He performed wonders before their ancestors In the land of Egypt, in the fields of Zoan.
He divided the sea and led them through, And He made the waters stand up like a wall.
Then He guided them with the cloud by day, And all night with a light of fire.
He split the rocks in the wilderness And gave them abundant water like the ocean depths.

He brought forth streams from the rock And made waters flow down like rivers. [Psalm 78 continues to verse 72; due to its length, would you like me to continue with now in Markdown, or pause here and pick up from verse
in the next message?] 17 Yet they continued to sin against Him, Rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
And they tested God in their hearts By demanding food to satisfy their cravings.
Then they spoke against God; They said, “Can God set a table in the wilderness?
Behold, He struck the rock so that waters gushed out, And streams overflowed; Can He also provide bread? Will He supply meat for His people?”
Therefore the Lord heard and was filled with wrath; And fire was kindled against Jacob, And anger also mounted against Israel,
Because they did not believe in God And did not trust in His deliverance.
Nevertheless, He commanded the skies above And opened the gates of heaven;
He rained down manna for them to eat, And gave them food from heaven.
Humanity partook of the bread of angels; He provided them with food in abundance.
He made the east wind blow in the heavens And by His power directed the south wind.
He rained meat upon them like dust, Even winged birds like the sand of the seas,
He allowed them to fall in the midst of their camp, All around their dwellings.
So they ate and were well satisfied, And He fulfilled their desire.
Yet before they had satisfied their craving, While their food was still in their mouths,
The anger of God rose against them And struck down some of their strongest, And subdued the choice men of Israel.
Despite all this, they continued to sin And did not believe in His marvellous works.
So He ended their days in futility, And their years in sudden terror.
When He struck them down, then they sought Him, And they returned and earnestly searched for God;
And they recalled that God was their rock, And the Most High God their Redeemer.
But they flattered Him with their words And lied to Him with their tongues.
For their hearts were not steadfast toward Him, Nor were they faithful to His covenant.
But He, being compassionate, forgave their iniquity and did not destroy them; And often He restrained His anger And did not unleash all His wrath.
So He remembered that they were mere flesh, A passing breeze that does not return.
How frequently they rebelled against Him in the wilderness, And grieved Him in the desert!
Time and again they tested God, And pained the Holy One of Israel.
They did not remember His power, The day when He redeemed them from the foe,
When He displayed His signs in Egypt And His wonders in the fields of Zoan,
And turned their rivers to blood, And their streams, so they could not drink.
He sent swarms of flies among them that devoured them, And frogs that devastated them.
He also gave their crops to the grasshopper And the produce of their labour to the locust.
He ruined their vines with hail And their sycamore trees with frost.
He turned their livestock over to the hailstones, And their herds to bolts of lightning.
He sent His burning anger upon them, Fury and indignation and trouble, A company of destroying angels.
He cleared a path for His anger; He did not spare their souls from death, But handed their lives over to the plague,
And struck all the firstborn in Egypt, The first and best of their strength in the tents of Ham.
Yet He led His own people out like sheep, And guided them in the wilderness like a flock;
He led them safely, so they did not fear; But the sea overwhelmed their foes.
So He brought them to His holy land, To this hill country which His right hand had won.
He also drove out the nations before them, And allotted them as an inheritance by measurement, And settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Yet they tested and rebelled against the Most High God And did not keep His decrees,
But turned back and acted deceitfully like their ancestors; They turned aside like a faulty bow.
For they provoked Him with their high places And stirred Him to jealousy with their carved images.
When God heard them, He was filled with wrath And He utterly rejected Israel;
So that He abandoned the dwelling place at Shiloh, The tent He had set among people,
And gave His strength into captivity, And His glory into the hand of the enemy.
He also delivered His people to the sword, And was filled with wrath at His inheritance.
Fire consumed His young men, And His maidens had no wedding songs.
His priests fell by the sword, And His widows could not weep.
Then the Lord awoke as if from sleep, Like a warrior overcome by wine.
He drove His enemies backward; He put on them an everlasting disgrace.
He also turned away from the tent of Joseph, And did not choose the tribe of Ephraim,
But selected the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which He cherished.
And He established His sanctuary like the heights, Like the earth which He has founded forever.
He also chose His servant David, And took him from tending the sheep;
From caring for the ewes with nursing lambs He brought him To shepherd Jacob His people, And Israel His inheritance.
So he shepherded them with integrity of heart, And guided them with skilful hands.
A Psalm of Asaph. God, the nations have invaded Your inheritance; They have defiled Your holy temple; They have laid Jerusalem in ruins.
They have given the dead bodies of Your servants as food for the birds of the sky, The flesh of Your godly ones to the animals of the earth.
They have shed their blood like water all around Jerusalem, And there was no one to lay them to rest.
We have become a disgrace before our neighbours, An object of ridicule and derision to those around us.
How long, Lord? Will Your anger stay forever? Will Your zealousness burn like fire?
Pour out Your wrath upon the nations that do not know You, And upon the kingdoms that do not call upon Your name.
For they have consumed Jacob And brought ruin to his dwelling place.
Do not hold us accountable for the wrongs of our ancestors; Let Your compassion swiftly meet us, For we have been brought very low.
Assist us, God of our salvation, for the honour of Your name; And deliver us and pardon our sins for the sake of Your name.
Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?” Let the retribution for the blood of Your servants that has been shed Be made known among the nations before our eyes.
Let the lament of the prisoner come before You; According to the greatness of Your power, let those destined to die be spared.
And return to our neighbours sevenfold into their lap Their taunts with which they have taunted You, Lord.
So we, Your people and the sheep of Your pasture, Will give thanks to You forever; To all generations, we will proclaim Your praise.
For the music director; set to Shoshannim, Eduth. A Psalm of Asaph. Listen, Shepherd of Israel, Who leads Joseph like a flock; You who are enthroned above the cherubim, shine forth!
Awaken Your might before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh, And come to our rescue!
God, bring us back And let Your face shine upon us, so we may be saved.
Lord God of hosts, How long will You be displeased with the prayers of Your people?
You have given them the bread of tears, And You have made them drink abundant tears.
You make us a source of strife to our neighbours, And our enemies laugh among themselves.
God of hosts, bring us back And let Your face shine upon us, so we may be saved.
You brought a vine out of Egypt; You displaced the nations and planted it.
You prepared the ground for it, And it took deep root and filled the land.
Its shadow covered the mountains, And its branches reached the mighty cedars.
It extended its branches to the sea And its shoots to the Euphrates River.
Why have You broken down its walls, So that all who pass that way pluck its fruit?
A boar from the forest ravages it, And whatever moves in the field feeds on it.
God of hosts, please return; Look down from heaven and see, and tend to this vine,
The root Your right hand has planted, And the son whom You have raised for Yourself.
It is consumed by fire, it is cut down; They perish at the rebuke of Your presence.
Let Your hand rest upon the man at Your right hand, Upon the son of man whom You have made strong for Yourself.
Then we will not turn away from You; Revive us, and we will call upon Your name.
Lord God of hosts, bring us back; Let Your face shine upon us, so we may be saved.
For the music director; on the Gittith. A Psalm of Asaph. Sing for joy to God our strength; Shout joyfully to the God of Jacob.
Raise a song, strike the tambourine, The sweet-sounding lyre with the harp.
Sound the trumpet at the new moon, At the full moon, on our day of celebration.
For it is a decree for Israel, A command of the God of Jacob.
He set it up as a witness in Joseph, When he journeyed through the land of Egypt; I heard a language unfamiliar to me:
“I took the burden from his shoulder, His hands were released from the basket.
You called in distress and I delivered you; I answered you from the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
Listen, My people, and I will warn you; Israel, if you would heed My voice!
Let there be no foreign god among you; Nor shall you bow down to an alien god.
I am the Lord your God, Who led you out of the land of Egypt; Open your mouth wide, and I will satisfy you.
Yet My people did not heed My voice, And Israel did not follow Me.
So I let them follow the stubbornness of their heart, To pursue their own devices.
Oh, that My people would heed Me, That Israel would follow My paths!
I would swiftly defeat their foes And turn My hand against their adversaries.
Those who despise the Lord would feign obedience, And their punishment would be everlasting.
But I would provide you with the finest wheat, And with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”
A Psalm of Asaph. God takes His stand in His own congregation; He judges in the midst of the gods.
How long will you render unjust judgments And favour the wicked? Selah
Defend the weak and fatherless; Uphold the rights of the afflicted and destitute.
Deliver the weak and needy; Rescue them from the grasp of the wicked.
They neither know nor understand; They wander in darkness; All the foundations of the earth are unstable.
I declared, “You are gods, And all of you are children of the Most High.
Yet you shall die like mortals, And fall like any other ruler.”
Rise up, God, and judge the earth!
For You have dominion over all the nations. A Song, a Psalm of Asaph. God, do not remain quiet; Do not be silent and, God, do not be still.
For see, Your enemies are in uproar, And those who oppose You have lifted themselves up.
They devise cunning schemes against Your people, And plot together against Your cherished ones.
They have declared, “Come, let us destroy them as a nation, So that the name of Israel will be forgotten.”
For they have united with a single purpose; They have formed an alliance against You:
The encampments of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites;
The people of Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia along with the inhabitants of Tyre;
Assyria too has allied with them; They have lent support to the descendants of Lot. Selah
Deal with them as with Midian, As with Sisera and Jabin at the river Kishon,
Who met their end at En-dor, Becoming refuse for the earth.
Let their nobles be as Oreb and Zeeb, And their leaders as Zebah and Zalmunna,
Who said, “Let us take possession Of the fields of God.”
My God, make them like the swirling dust, Like chaff driven by the wind.
Like a fire that consumes the forest, And like a flame that ignites the mountains,
So chase them with Your tempest, And frighten them with Your storm.
Cover their faces with shame, So that they may seek Your name, Lord.
May they be eternally ashamed and dismayed, And may they be disgraced and vanish,
So that they will recognise that You alone, whose name is the Lord, Are the Most High over all the earth.
For the music director; on the Gittith. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. How lovely are Your dwelling places, Lord of armies!
My soul longs and even yearns for the courtyards of the Lord; My heart and my flesh rejoice in the living God.
The bird also finds a house, And the swallow a nest for herself, where she may place her young: Your altars, Lord of armies, My King and my God.
Happy are those who reside in Your house! They continually praise You. Selah
Fortunate is the person whose strength is in You, In whose heart are the paths to Zion!
As they pass through the Valley of Baca, they make it a place of springs; The early rain also covers it with blessings.
They progress from strength to strength, Each one appears before God in Zion.
Lord God of armies, hear my prayer; Attend, God of Jacob! Selah
Behold our shield, God, And regard the face of Your anointed.
For a day in Your courtyards is more desirable than a thousand elsewhere. I would prefer to stand at the threshold of the house of my God Than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.
For the Lord God is a sun and shield; The Lord bestows grace and honour; He withholds no good thing from those who walk with integrity.
Lord of armies, Fortunate is the person who places their trust in You!
For the music director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Lord, You showed favour to Your land; You restored the fortunes of Jacob.
You pardoned the wrongdoing of Your people; You covered all their sin. Selah
You withdrew all Your fury; You turned away from Your blazing wrath.
Renew us, God of our salvation, And let Your indignation toward us come to an end.
Will You remain angry with us forever? Will Your anger extend to all generations?
Will You not bring us to life again, So that Your people may find joy in You?
Reveal Your mercy to us, Lord, And grant us Your salvation.
I will listen to what God the Lord will say; For He will speak peace to His people, to His faithful ones; And may they not return to folly.
Surely His salvation is near to those who revere Him, That glory may reside in our land.
Mercy and truth have met together; Righteousness and peace have embraced each other.
Truth springs up from the earth, And righteousness gazes down from heaven.
Indeed, the Lord will provide what is good, And our land will yield its harvest.
Righteousness will precede Him And will prepare a path for His steps.
A Prayer of David. Incline Your ear, Lord, and answer me; For I am afflicted and needy.
Guard my soul, for I am devoted to You; You are my God, deliver Your servant who trusts in You.
Be merciful to me, Lord, For I call upon You throughout the day.
Bring joy to the soul of Your servant, For to You, Lord, I lift up my soul.
For You, Lord, are good, and ready to pardon, And rich in mercy to all who call upon You.
Hear, Lord, my prayer; And attend to the sound of my supplications!
In the day of my trouble I will call upon You, For You will respond to me.
There is none like You among the gods, Lord, Nor are there any deeds like Yours.
All nations You have made will come and worship before You, Lord, And they will honour Your name.
For You are great, and You perform marvellous deeds; You alone are God.
Instruct me in Your way, Lord; I will walk in Your truth; Unite my heart to revere Your name.
I will give thanks to You, Lord my God, with all my heart, And I will honour Your name forever.
For Your mercy toward me is immense, And You have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
God, proud men have risen up against me, And a group of violent men have sought my life, And they have not considered You.
But You, Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, Slow to anger and rich in mercy and truth.
Turn to me, and be merciful to me; Grant Your strength to Your servant, And save the child of Your maidservant.
Show me a sign of Your goodness, That those who hate me may see it and be ashamed, Because You, Lord, have helped me and comforted me.
A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A Song. His foundation is set in the holy mountains.
The Lord cherishes the gates of Zion More than all the other homes of Jacob.
Wonderful things are declared about you, City of God. Selah
“I will mention Rahab and Babylon among those who know Me; Behold, Philistia and Tyre with Cush: ‘This one was born there.’”
Yet of Zion it will be proclaimed, “This one and that one were born in her”; And the Most High Himself will uphold her.
The Lord will take account when He records the peoples: “This one was born there.” Selah
Then those who sing, along with those who play the flutes, will proclaim, “All my sources of joy are in you.”

A Song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. For the music director; according to Mahalath Leannoth. A Maskil of Heman the Ezrahite. Lord, God of my salvation, I have cried out by day and in the night before You.
May my prayer reach You; Please listen to my plea!
For my soul is overwhelmed with troubles, And my life draws near to Sheol.
I am regarded as one who descends to the pit; I have become like a person without strength,
Abandoned among the dead, Like the slain who lie in the grave, Whom You remember no more, And they are cut off from Your hand.
You have placed me in the lowest pit, In dark places, in the depths.
Your wrath has weighed heavily upon me, And You have overwhelmed me with all Your waves. Selah
You have distanced my acquaintances from me; You have made me an object of loathing to them; I am shut in and cannot go out.
My eye has wasted away because of misery; I have called upon You every day, Lord; I have spread out my hands to You.
Will You work wonders for the dead? Or will the departed spirits rise and praise You? Selah
Will Your mercy be proclaimed in the grave, Your faithfulness in Abaddon?
Will Your wonders be revealed in the darkness? And Your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
But I, Lord, have cried out to You for help, And in the morning my prayer reaches You.
Lord, why do You turn away from my soul? Why do You hide Your face from me?
I have been afflicted and near death from my youth; I endure Your terrors; I grow weary.
Your fierce anger has swept over me; Your terrors have undone me.
They have surrounded me like water all day long; They have encircled me completely.
You have taken lover and friend far from me; My acquaintances dwell in darkness.
A Maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the mercy of the Lord forever; With my mouth I will make Your faithfulness known through all generations.
For I have said, “Mercy will be built up forever; In the heavens You will establish Your faithfulness.”
“I have made a promise with My chosen one; I have pledged to My servant David,
I will secure your descendants for all time, And uphold your throne through every generation.” Selah
The heavens will celebrate Your wonders, Lord, Your faithfulness also among the assembly of the holy ones.
For who in the skies can be compared to the Lord? Who among the mighty is like the Lord,
A God greatly revered in the council of the holy ones, And awe-inspiring above all who surround Him?
Lord God of hosts, who is like You, mighty Lord? Your faithfulness encircles You.
You govern the surging of the sea; When its waves rise, You still them.
You have crushed Rahab like one who is slain; You scattered Your foes with Your powerful arm.
The heavens belong to You, and the earth is Yours as well; You have established the world and all it contains.
You have fashioned the north and the south; Tabor and Hermon sing for joy in Your name.
You possess a strong arm; Your hand is mighty, Your right hand is lifted high.
Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne; Mercy and truth proceed before You.
Blessed are the people who hear the joyful call! Lord, they walk in the light of Your presence.
In Your name they rejoice all day long, And by Your righteousness they are uplifted.
For You are the splendour of their strength, And by Your favour our horn is raised.
For our protector is the Lord, And our sovereign is the Holy One of Israel.
Once You spoke in a vision to Your faithful ones, And said, “I have granted help to a mighty one; I have elevated one chosen from among the people.
I have discovered My servant David; With My sacred oil I have anointed him,
With whom My hand will be firm; My arm also will empower him.
The enemy will not outwit him, Nor will the wicked oppress him.
But I will crush his foes before him, And strike down those who detest him.
My faithfulness and My mercy will accompany him, And in My name his horn will be raised.
I will extend his hand over the sea, And his right hand over the rivers.
He will call to Me, ‘You are my Father, My God, and the foundation of my salvation.’
I will also appoint him as My firstborn, The highest of the kings of the earth.
I will maintain My mercy for him forever, And My covenant shall be steadfast with him.
Thus I will secure his descendants for all time, And his throne as enduring as the days of heaven.
If his sons forsake My Law And do not follow My judgments,
If they break My statutes And do not observe My commandments,
Then I will discipline their wrongdoing with the rod, And their guilt with afflictions.
But I will not withdraw My mercy from him, Nor be unfaithful to My promise.
I will not violate My covenant, Nor alter what has gone out of My lips.
Once I have sworn by My holiness; I will not deceive David.
His descendants shall last forever, And his throne as constant as the sun before Me.
It shall be established forever like the moon, And a faithful witness in the sky.” Selah
But You have rejected and spurned, You have been full of wrath against Your anointed.
You have renounced the covenant of Your servant; You have defiled his crown in the dust.
You have breached all his walls; You have reduced his strongholds to ruin.
All who pass by plunder him; He has become a disgrace to his neighbours.
You have elevated the right hand of his adversaries; You have made all his enemies rejoice.
You also turn back the edge of his sword, And have not supported him in battle.
You have brought an end to his splendour And cast his throne to the ground.
You have shortened the days of his youth; You have enveloped him in dishonour. Selah
How long, Lord? Will You hide Yourself perpetually? Will Your wrath blaze like fire?
Remember how fleeting my life is; For what futility You have created all the sons of mankind!
What man can live and not see death? Can he save his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah
Where are Your former acts of mercy, Lord, Which You pledged to David in Your faithfulness?
Remember, Lord, the taunt against Your servants; How I bear in my heart the taunts of all the many peoples,
With which Your enemies have taunted, Lord, With which they have mocked the footsteps of Your anointed.
May the Lord be glorified eternally!
Amen and Amen. A Prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, You have been our dwelling place in all generations.
Before the mountains came into being Or You formed the earth and the world, Even from eternity to eternity, You are God.
You return mortals to dust And declare, “Return, you children of humanity.”
For a thousand years in Your sight Are like a day that has just gone by, Or like a watch in the night.
You carry them away like a flood, they fall asleep; In the morning they are like grass that renews itself.
In the morning it flourishes and grows anew; By evening it fades and withers away.
For we have been consumed by Your anger, And we have been dismayed by Your wrath.
You have placed our wrongdoings before You, Our hidden sins in the light of Your presence.
For all our days have passed away under Your fury; We have ended our years with a sigh.
As for the days of our life, they contain seventy years, Or if due to strength, eighty years, Yet their pride is only trouble and sorrow; For it quickly passes, and we disappear.
Who comprehends the power of Your anger And Your wrath, according to the reverence that is due You?
So guide us to count our days rightly, That we may develop a heart of wisdom.
Return, Lord; how long will it be? And have compassion on Your servants.
Satisfy us in the morning with Your mercy, That we may sing for joy and be glad all our days.
Make us joyful according to the days You have afflicted us, And the years we have experienced adversity.
Let Your deeds be shown to Your servants And Your splendour to their children.
May the favour of the Lord our God rest upon us; And establish for us the work of our hands— Yes, establish the work of our hands.
Whoever resides in the refuge of the Most High Will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
I will declare of the Lord, “You are my refuge and my fortress, My God, in whom I place my trust!”
For He is the one who delivers you from the snare of the trapper And from the perilous plague.
He will shield you with His feathers, And beneath His wings you may find refuge; His faithfulness is a protective shield and barrier.
You need not fear the terror of the night, Nor the arrow that flies by day;
Nor the pestilence that prowls in darkness, Or the destruction that lays waste at noon.
Though a thousand may fall at your side And ten thousand at your right hand, It shall not come near you.
You will merely observe with your eyes And witness the recompense of the wicked.
Since you have made the Lord your refuge, The Most High your dwelling place,
No harm will overtake you, Nor will any plague come near your dwelling.
For He will give His angels charge over you, To watch over you in all your ways.
They will bear you up in their hands, So that you will not dash your foot against a stone.
You will tread upon the lion and the cobra, You will trample the fierce lion and the serpent.
“Because he holds fast to Me in love, I will deliver him; I will set him on high, because he acknowledges My name.
He will call upon Me, and I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble; I will rescue him and honour him.
I will bless him with a long life, And reveal to him My salvation.”
A Psalm, a Song for the Sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to the Lord And to sing praises to Your name, Most High;
To declare Your mercy in the morning And Your faithfulness by night,
With harmonious sounds of the lute and harp, With melodious music on the lyre.
For You, Lord, have filled me with joy through Your deeds, I will sing with joy over the works of Your hands.
How magnificent are Your works, Lord! Your thoughts are profoundly deep.
An unwise person lacks knowledge, Nor does a senseless person comprehend this:
When the wicked sprouted up like grass And all who did injustice flourished, It was only that they might be destroyed eternally.
But You, Lord, are forever on high.
For indeed, Your enemies, Lord, For indeed, Your enemies will perish; All who do injustice will be scattered.
But You have lifted my horn like that of the wild ox; I have been anointed with fresh oil.
And my eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies, My ears hear of the evildoers who rise up against me.
The righteous will thrive like the palm tree, They will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
Rooted in the house of the Lord, They will flourish in the courts of our God.
They will still bear fruit in their later years; They will be full of vitality and very green,
To declare that the Lord is upright; He is my rock, and there is no malice in Him.
The Lord reigns, adorned in majesty; The Lord has wrapped Himself in strength. Truly, the world is firmly established; it shall not be moved.
Your throne has been established from ancient times; You are everlasting.
The floods have risen, Lord, The floods have raised their voice, The floods lift up their crashing waves.
Beyond the sounds of many waters, Beyond the mighty breakers of the sea, The Lord on high is powerful.
Your decrees are thoroughly affirmed; Holiness adorns Your house, Lord, for all time.
Lord, God who brings justice, God who brings justice, reveal Yourself!
Arise, Judge of the earth, Render justice to the proud.
How long, Lord, shall the wicked— How long shall the wicked prevail?
They pour out words, they speak with arrogance; All who do wrong boast.
They oppress Your people, Lord, And bring harm to Your inheritance.
They take the lives of the widow and the stranger, And slay the orphans.
They have said, “The Lord does not observe, Nor does the God of Jacob notice.”
Pay attention, you senseless ones among the people; And when will you understand, foolish ones?
He who created the ear, does He not hear? He who shaped the eye, does He not see?
He who instructs the nations, will He not correct, He who imparts knowledge to mankind?
The Lord knows the thoughts of mankind, That they are but a fleeting breath.
Blessed is the one whom You correct, Lord, And whom You instruct from Your Law,
So that You may provide him relief from the days of trouble, Until a pit is prepared for the wicked.
For the Lord will not desert His people, Nor will He abandon His inheritance.
For justice will once more be fair, And all the upright in heart will pursue it.
Who will rise up for me against evildoers? Who will stand with me against those who do wrong?
If the Lord had not been my support, My soul would soon have dwelt in silence.
If I should say, “My foot has slipped,” Your mercy, Lord, will sustain me.
When my anxious thoughts increase within me, Your comfort brings joy to my soul.
Can a throne of destruction be associated with You, One which plans mischief by decree?
They gather against the life of the righteous And condemn the innocent to death.
But the Lord has been my refuge, And my God the rock of my protection.
He has returned their wrongdoing upon them, And He will bring them to ruin in their wickedness; The Lord our God will bring them to ruin.
Come, let us sing with joy to the Lord, Let us shout with gladness to the rock of our salvation.
Let us approach His presence with a song of gratitude, Let us joyfully praise Him with music.
For the Lord is indeed a great God And a mighty King above all gods,
In His hand are the depths of the earth, The peaks of the mountains belong to Him as well.
The sea is His, for He created it, And His hands shaped the dry land.
Come, let us worship and bow down, Let us kneel before the Lord, our Creator.
For He is our God, And we are His people, the flock He tends. Today, if you hear His voice,
Do not harden your hearts as at Meribah, Like on the day at Massah in the wilderness,
When your ancestors tested Me, They tried Me, though they had seen My deeds.
For forty years I was grieved with that generation, And said they are a people who go astray in their heart, And they have not understood My ways.
Therefore I declared in My wrath, They shall certainly not enter My rest.
Sing a fresh song to the Lord; Sing to the Lord, everyone on earth.
Sing to the Lord, bless His name; Announce the good news of His salvation every day.
Proclaim His glory among the nations, His wonderful deeds among all the peoples.
For the Lord is great and worthy of much praise; He is to be revered above all gods.
For all the gods of the peoples are mere idols, But the Lord created the heavens.
Splendour and majesty are in His presence, Strength and beauty are found in His sanctuary.
Attribute to the Lord, you families of nations, Attribute to the Lord glory and strength.
Attribute to the Lord the glory due His name; Bring an offering and enter His courtyards.
Worship the Lord in holy attire; Tremble before Him, everyone on earth.
Declare among the nations, “The Lord reigns; Indeed, the world is firmly established, it shall not be shaken; He will judge the peoples with fairness.”
Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; Let the sea resound, and all that is in it;
Let the field be jubilant, and everything in it; Then all the trees of the forest will sing with joy
In the presence of the Lord, for He is coming, For He is coming to judge the earth.
He will judge the world with righteousness, And the peoples with His faithfulness. The Lord reigns, let the earth be glad; Let the many islands celebrate.
Clouds and deep darkness surround Him; Righteousness and justice form the base of His throne.
Fire advances before Him And consumes His foes on every side.
His lightning illuminated the world; The earth saw it and shook.
The mountains dissolved like wax in the presence of the Lord, In the presence of the Lord of all the earth.
The heavens proclaim His righteousness, And all the peoples have witnessed His glory.
Let all those who serve carved images be put to shame, Who take pride in idols; Bow down to Him, all you gods.
Zion heard and was glad, And the daughters of Judah have rejoiced Because of Your judgments, Lord.
For You are the Lord Most High over all the earth; You are elevated far above all gods.
Detest evil, you who love the Lord, Who guards the souls of His faithful ones; He delivers them from the grasp of the wicked.
Light is scattered for the righteous, And joy for the upright in heart.
Rejoice in the Lord, you righteous ones, And praise His holy name.
A Psalm. Sing a new song to the Lord, For He has done marvellous things, His right hand and His holy arm have gained the victory for Him.
The Lord has made His salvation known; He has shown His righteousness to all the nations.
He has remembered His mercy and His faithfulness to the house of Israel; All the ends of the earth have witnessed the salvation of our God.
Shout joyfully to the Lord, all the earth; Be glad and sing for joy and offer praises.
Sing praises to the Lord with the lyre, With the lyre and the sound of a melody.
With trumpets and the sound of the horn Rejoice before the King, the Lord.
Let the sea roar and all it holds, The world, and those who inhabit it.
Let the rivers applaud with joy, Let the mountains join in joyful song
In the presence of the Lord, for He is coming to judge the earth; He will judge the world with righteousness, And the peoples with fairness.
The Lord reigns, let the peoples tremble; He sits enthroned above the cherubim, let the earth shake.
The Lord is great in Zion, And He is lifted high above all the peoples.
Let them praise Your great and awe-inspiring name; Holy is He.
The strength of the King loves justice; You have established fairness; You have executed justice and righteousness in Jacob.
Lift up the Lord our God And worship at His footstool; Holy is He.
Among His priests were Moses and Aaron, And Samuel was among those who invoked His name; They called upon the Lord, and He responded to them.
He spoke to them from the pillar of cloud; They kept His testimonies And the statute that He provided them.
Lord our God, You responded to them; You were a forgiving God to them, Yet an avenger of their misdeeds.
Lift up the Lord our God And worship at His holy hill, For the Lord our God is holy.
A Psalm of thanksgiving. Shout with joy to the Lord, all the earth.
Serve the Lord with gladness; Approach Him with joyful singing.
Recognise that the Lord is indeed God; He is our Creator, and we did not create ourselves; We belong to Him, the flock under His care.
Enter His gates with gratitude, And His courts with praise. Offer thanks to Him, honour His name.
For the Lord is good; His kindness endures forever, And His faithfulness continues through all generations.
A Psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and justice; To You, Lord, I will sing praises.
I will strive to follow the path of integrity. When will You visit me? I will walk within the halls of my house with a sincere heart.
I will place nothing worthless before my eyes; I despise the deeds of those who stray; It shall not cling to me.
A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will not engage in evil.
Whoever secretly slanders his neighbour, I will put an end to him; The one who has a haughty look and an arrogant heart, I will not endure.
My eyes will be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; One who walks in a blameless way is the one who will serve me.
One who practices deceit shall not remain within my house; One who speaks lies shall not stand firm before me.
Every morning I will remove all the wicked of the land, So as to rid the city of the Lord of all those who commit injustice.
A Prayer of the afflicted when he is weak and pours out his complaint before the Lord. Hear my prayer, Lord! And let my cry for help come to You.
Do not hide Your face from me on the day of my distress; Incline Your ear to me; On the day I call, answer me quickly.
For my days have vanished like smoke, And my bones feel scorched as in a hearth.
My heart is stricken and withered like grass, Indeed, I forget to eat my bread.
Due to my intense groaning, My bones cling to my flesh.
I am like a pelican in the wilderness; I have become like an owl among the ruins.
I lie awake, I am like a solitary bird on a housetop.
My enemies have taunted me all day long; Those who ridicule me use my name as a curse.
For I have eaten ashes as if they were bread, And mingled my drink with tears
Because of Your indignation and Your wrath; For You have picked me up and thrown me away.
My days are like an extended shadow, And I wither away like grass.
But You, Lord, endure forever, And Your name is remembered through all generations.
You will arise and have mercy on Zion; For it is time to show her favour, For the appointed time has come.
Surely Your servants cherish her stones, And have compassion on her dust.
So the nations will revere the name of the Lord, And all the kings of the earth will see Your glory.
For the Lord has rebuilt Zion; He has revealed His glory.
He has heeded the prayer of the destitute, And has not disregarded their plea.
This will be recorded for future generations, So that a people yet to be created may praise the Lord:
For He gazed down from His holy height; From heaven the Lord observed the earth,
To listen to the groans of the prisoners, To free those condemned to death,
So that people may proclaim the name of the Lord in Zion, And His praise in Jerusalem,
When the peoples are gathered together, And the kingdoms, to worship the Lord.
He has weakened my strength along the way; He has shortened my days.
I say, “My God, do not take me away in the midst of my days, Your years extend through all generations.
In times past, You established the earth's foundation, And the heavens are crafted by Your hands.
They will perish, but You remain; All of them will wear out like a garment; Like a ruined cloth You will change them, and they will be gone.
But You remain unchanged, And Your years will never end.
The children of Your servants will dwell securely, And their descendants will be established before You.”
Of David. Bless the Lord, my soul, And all that is within me, bless His holy name.
Praise the Lord, my soul, And remember all His benefits;
Who forgives all your iniquities, Who heals all your ailments;
Who redeems your life from the pit, Who crowns you with favour and compassion;
Who fills your life with good things, So that your youth is renewed like the eagle.
The Lord executes righteous acts And justice for all who are oppressed.
He revealed His ways to Moses, His deeds to the children of Israel.
The Lord is compassionate and gracious, Slow to anger and rich in mercy.
He will not always accuse us, Nor will He harbour His anger forever.
He has not treated us as our sins deserve, Nor repaid us according to our iniquities.
For as high as the heavens are above the earth, So great is His mercy towards those who fear Him.
As far as the east is from the west, So far has He taken away our transgressions from us.
Just as a father shows compassion to his children, So the Lord shows compassion to those who fear Him.
For He understands our nature; He remembers that we are mere dust.
As for man, his days are like grass; Like a flower of the field, so he blooms.
When the wind has passed over it, it is no more, And its place remembers it no longer.
But the mercy of the Lord is from everlasting to everlasting Upon those who fear Him, And His righteousness to the children’s children,
To those who uphold His covenant And remember His commandments to follow them.
The Lord has set His throne in the heavens, And His kingdom reigns over all.
Praise the Lord, you His angels, Mighty in strength, who carry out His word, Obeying the voice of His word!
Praise the Lord, all you His hosts, You who serve Him, doing His will.
Praise the Lord, all you works of His, In all places of His dominion; Praise the Lord, my soul!
Bless the Lord, my soul! Lord my God, You are very great; You are clothed with splendour and majesty,
Wrapping Yourself in light as with a garment, Spreading out the heavens like a tent curtain.
He constructs the beams of His upper chambers on the waters; He uses the clouds as His chariot; He moves upon the wings of the wind;
He appoints the winds as His messengers, Flaming fire as His servants.
He founded the earth upon its bases, So that it will not be shaken for all time.
You enveloped it with the deep as with a covering; The waters stood above the mountains.
At Your rebuke they retreated, At the sound of Your thunder they rushed away.
The mountains ascended, the valleys descended To the place You designated for them.
You set a limit so that they will not cross, So that they will not return to flood the earth.
He releases springs into the valleys; They travel among the mountains;
They provide drink to every beast of the field; The wild donkeys satisfy their thirst.
Beside them the birds of the sky reside; They sing among the branches.
He irrigates the mountains from His upper chambers; The earth is fulfilled with the yield of His works.
He causes the grass to sprout for the cattle, And plants for the labour of mankind, So that they may bring forth food from the earth,
And wine, which gladdens a human heart, So that one's face shines with oil, And food, which strengthens a human heart.
The trees of the Lord are well-watered, The cedars of Lebanon which He established,
Where the birds make their nests, And the stork, whose dwelling is the fir trees.
The high mountains are for the wild goats; The cliffs serve as a refuge for the rock hyrax.
He created the moon to mark the seasons; The sun knows its setting place.
You bring about darkness and it becomes night, In which all the creatures of the forest move about.
The young lions roar for their prey, And seek their sustenance from God.
When the sun rises, they retreat, And lie down in their dens.
A person goes out to his work And to his labour until evening.
Lord, how numerous are Your works! In wisdom You have crafted them all; The earth is filled with Your creations.
This is the sea, vast and expansive, In which are countless creatures, Beings both small and great.
The ships navigate there, And Leviathan, which You have fashioned to frolic in it.
They all depend on You They wait for their food to be given in time.
You offer to them, they gather it; You open Your hand, they are filled with good things.
You hide Your face, they are dismayed; You withdraw their breath, they expire And return to their dust.
You send forth Your Spirit, they are brought into being; And You renew the surface of the ground.
May the glory of the Lord last forever; May the Lord take delight in His creations;
He observes the earth, and it quakes; He touches the mountains, and they emit smoke.
