I will sing to the Lord throughout my life; I will sing praise to my God while I exist.
May my meditation be pleasing to Him; As for me, I shall find joy in the Lord.
May sinners vanish from the earth And may the wicked be no more.
Bless the Lord, my soul. Praise the Lord! Offer gratitude to the Lord, call upon His name; Proclaim His deeds among the peoples.
Sing to Him, offer praises to Him; Declare all His wonders.
Glory in His holy name; May the heart of those who seek the Lord be joyful.
Seek the Lord and His strength; Pursue His presence continually.
Recall His wonders which He has performed, His marvels, and the judgments spoken by His mouth,
You descendants of His servant Abraham, You sons of Jacob, His chosen ones!
He is the Lord our God; His judgments are throughout the earth.
He has remembered His covenant eternally, The word which He commanded to a thousand generations,
The covenant which He established with Abraham, And His promise to Isaac.
Then He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree, To Israel as an everlasting covenant,
Declaring, “To you I will give the land of Canaan As your allotted inheritance.”
When they were but a few in number, Very few, and strangers in it,
And they moved from nation to nation, From one kingdom to another people,
He permitted no one to oppress them, And reproved kings on their behalf, declaring,
“Do not touch My anointed ones, And do no harm to My prophets.”
And He summoned a famine upon the land; He broke the entire supply of bread.
He sent a man ahead of them, Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
They afflicted his feet with shackles, He himself was put in irons;
Until the time his word was fulfilled, The word of the Lord tested him.
The king called for him and set him free, The ruler of peoples released him.
He appointed him lord of his household, And ruler over all his possessions,
To bind his officials at will, That he might teach his elders wisdom.
Israel also came into Egypt; So Jacob resided in the land of Ham.
And He made His people very fruitful, And made them stronger than their adversaries.
He turned their hearts to hate His people, To deal cunningly with His servants.
He sent Moses, His chosen servant, And Aaron, whom He had selected.
They displayed His miraculous signs among them, And wonders in the land of Ham.
He sent darkness and made it dark; And they did not defy His commands.
He turned their waters into blood, And caused their fish to perish.
Their land teemed with frogs Even in the chambers of their kings.
He spoke, and swarms of flies came, And gnats throughout their territory.
He gave them hail instead of rain, And flaming fire in their land.
He also struck down their vines and their fig trees, And shattered the trees of their territory.
He spoke, and locusts came, And countless grasshoppers beyond number,
And they devoured all the vegetation in their land, And consumed the fruit of their ground.
He also struck down all the firstborn in their land, The first fruits of all their strength.
Then He led the Israelites out with silver and gold, And among His tribes there was not one who faltered.
Egypt rejoiced when they departed, For fear of them had fallen upon the Egyptians.
He spread a cloud as a covering, And fire to illuminate the night.
They asked, and He brought quail, And provided them with the bread of heaven.
He opened the rock and water poured forth; It flowed like a river through the desert.
For He remembered His sacred pledge To His servant Abraham;
And He brought out His people with joy, His chosen ones with a joyful shout.
And He gave them the lands of the nations, And they inherited the labour of the peoples,
So that they might keep His statutes And comply with His laws.
Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord! Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good; For His mercy is everlasting.
Who can speak of the mighty deeds of the Lord, Or proclaim all His praise?
Blessed are those who keep justice, Who practise righteousness at all times!
Remember me, Lord, in Your favour toward Your people; Visit me with Your salvation,
That I may witness the prosperity of Your chosen ones, That I may share in the joy of Your nation, That I may take pride in Your inheritance.
We have sinned as our ancestors did, We have strayed, we have acted wickedly.
Our ancestors in Egypt did not grasp Your wonders; They failed to remember Your abundant kindnesses, But rebelled by the sea, at the Red Sea.
Yet He saved them for the sake of His name, To make His power known.
He rebuked the Red Sea and it dried up, And He led them through the mighty waters, as through the wilderness.
Thus He saved them from the hand of those who hated them, And redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
The waters covered their adversaries; Not one of them remained.
Then they trusted His words; They sang His praise.
They quickly forgot His works; They did not wait for His guidance,
But became greedily desirous in the wilderness, And tested God in the desert.
So He granted their request, But sent a wasting disease among them.
When they envied Moses in the camp, And Aaron, the holy one of the Lord,
The ground opened and took in Dathan, And enveloped the assembly of Abiram.
And a fire blazed up in their company; The flame consumed the evildoers.
They made a calf at Horeb, And worshipped a cast metal image.
Thus they exchanged their glory For the likeness of an ox that eats grass.
They forgot God their Saviour, Who had accomplished great things in Egypt,
Miracles in the land of Ham, And awe-inspiring deeds by the Red Sea.
Therefore He declared He would destroy them, Had not Moses, His chosen one, stood in the breach before Him, To turn away His wrath from destroying them.
Then they rejected the pleasant land; They did not believe His promise,
But grumbled in their tents; They did not heed the voice of the Lord.
Therefore He swore to them That He would let them fall in the wilderness,
And that He would disperse their descendants among the nations, And scatter them in the lands.
They also followed Baal of Peor, And ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
Thus they provoked Him to anger with their deeds, And a plague broke out among them.
Then Phinehas stood up and intervened, And the plague was halted.
And it was credited to him as righteousness, For all generations to come.
They also provoked Him to wrath at the waters of Meribah, So that it went badly for Moses because of them;
Because they defied His Spirit, He spoke rashly with his lips.
They did not eliminate the peoples, As the Lord had commanded them,
But they mixed with the nations And adopted their customs,
And worshipped their idols, Which became a trap for them.
For they even sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons,
And shed innocent blood, The blood of their sons and daughters, Whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; And the land was defiled with the blood.
Thus they became unclean in their practices, And were unfaithful in their deeds.
Therefore the anger of the Lord was kindled against His people, And He loathed His inheritance.
So He delivered them into the hands of the nations, And those who hated them ruled over them.
Their enemies also oppressed them, And they were subdued under their power.
Many times He would rescue them; Yet they were rebellious in their intentions, And they sank down into their guilt.
Yet He looked upon their distress When He heard their cry;
And He remembered His covenant for their sake, And relented according to the greatness of His mercy.
He also made them objects of compassion In the sight of all their captors.
Deliver us, Lord our God, And gather us from the nations, To give thanks to Your holy name And glory in Your praise.
Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, From everlasting to everlasting.
And all the people shall say, “Amen.” Praise the Lord! Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good; For His love is everlasting.
Let those redeemed by the Lord declare it, Those He has rescued from the hand of the adversary.
And brought together from distant lands, From the east and from the west, From the north and from the south.
They wandered in the wilderness, a desert region; They could not find a path to a city where they might dwell.
They were hungry and thirsty; Their souls grew faint within them.
Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble; He delivered them from their distresses.
He also led them by a straight way, To go to an inhabited city.
Let them give thanks to the Lord for His mercy, And for His marvellous deeds to the children of mankind!
For He has satisfied the thirsty soul, And He has filled the hungry soul with good things.
There were those who dwelt in darkness and in the shadow of death, Prisoners in suffering and chains,
Because they had rebelled against the words of God And rejected the counsel of the Most High.
Therefore He humbled their heart with labour; They stumbled and there was no one to help.
Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble; He rescued them from their distresses.
He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death And broke their chains apart.
Let them give thanks to the Lord for His mercy, And for His marvellous deeds to the children of mankind!
For He has shattered gates of bronze, And cut through bars of iron.
Fools, because of their rebellious ways, And because of their wrongdoings, were afflicted.
Their souls loathed all kinds of food, And they drew near to the gates of death.
Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble; He rescued them from their distresses.
He sent His word and healed them, And saved them from their ruin.
Let them give thanks to the Lord for His mercy, And for His marvellous deeds to the children of mankind!
Let them also bring offerings of gratitude, And proclaim His works with joyful singing.
Those who go down to the sea in ships, Who conduct business on great waters,
They have witnessed the works of the Lord, And His wonders in the deep.
For He spoke and stirred up a stormy wind, Which lifted the waves of the sea.
They rose up to the heavens, they went down to the depths; Their courage melted away in their distress.
They reeled and staggered like someone overcome with drink, And were at their wits’ end.
Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble, And He delivered them from their distresses.
He calmed the storm to a whisper, So that the waves of the sea were hushed.
Then they were glad, because they were quiet, So He guided them to their desired harbour.
Let them give thanks to the Lord for His mercy, And for His marvellous deeds to the children of mankind!
Let them also exalt Him in the congregation of the people, And praise Him at the assembly of the elders.
He turns rivers into a wilderness, And springs of water into parched ground;
A fruitful land into a barren wasteland, Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
He transforms a wilderness into a pool of water, And a dry land into springs of water;
And He has the hungry live there, So that they may establish a city to dwell in,
And cultivate fields and plant vineyards, And gather a fruitful harvest.
He also blesses them and they multiply greatly, And He does not let their cattle diminish.
When they are reduced and brought low Due to oppression, misery, and sorrow,
He pours contempt upon noblemen And makes them wander in a trackless wasteland.
But He sets the needy securely on high, away from affliction, And makes their families like a flock.
The upright see it and are glad; But all injustice is silenced.
Who is wise?
Let him pay attention to these things, And consider the mercy of the Lord. A Song. A Psalm of David. My heart is steadfast, God; I will sing, I will sing praises also with my soul.
Awake, harp and lyre; I will herald the dawn!
I will offer my thanks to You, Lord, among the peoples, And I will sing praises to You among the nations.
For Your mercy is vast above the heavens, And Your truth extends to the skies.
Be exalted high above the heavens, God, And may Your glory be over all the earth.
So that Your beloved may be delivered, Save with Your right hand, and respond to me!
God has spoken in His holiness: “I will triumph, I will divide Shechem, and measure out the Valley of Succoth.
Gilead is Mine, Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim also is the helmet of My head; Judah is My sceptre.
Moab is My washbowl; I will cast My sandal over Edom; I will proclaim victory over Philistia.”
Who will guide me into the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Have You Yourself not rejected us, God? And will You not go out with our armies?
Give us help against the adversary, For rescue by man is futile.
Through God we will achieve great deeds, And it is He who will subdue our foes.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. God of my praise, do not be silent!
For they have spoken with malice and deceit against me; They have used false words against me.
They have surrounded me with words of hatred, And fought against me without cause.
In return for my love they accuse me; But I continue in prayer.
Thus they have repaid me evil for good, And animosity for my love.
Let an unrighteous person be appointed over him, And may a challenger stand at his right hand.
When he is judged, may he be found guilty, And may his prayer be considered as sin.
May his days be few; May another assume his position.
May his children be left without a father, And his wife without a husband.
May his children wander and seek help; And may they search for sustenance far from their devastated homes.
May the creditor seize all that he has, And may strangers take the fruits of his labour.
May there be no one to show him kindness, Nor any to show favour to his orphaned children.
May his descendants be cut off; May their name be erased in the next generation.
May the wrongdoing of his ancestors be remembered before the Lord, And do not let the sin of his mother be erased.
May they be before the Lord continually, So that He may remove their memory from the earth;
Because he failed to show compassion, But pursued the afflicted and needy, And the downhearted, to bring about their demise.
He also embraced cursing, so it befell him; And he did not take pleasure in blessing, so it was distant from him.
He also wore cursing like a garment, And it entered his body like water, And like oil into his bones.
May it be to him like a garment he wraps around himself, And like a belt he constantly wears.
May this be the recompense of my accusers from the Lord, And of those who speak harm against my soul.
But You, O God, the Lord, act kindly towards me for the sake of Your name; Because Your mercy is good, deliver me;
For I am afflicted and in need, And my heart is deeply wounded.
I am fading away like a lengthening shadow; I am tossed aside like the locust.
My knees are weakened from fasting, And my body has become lean, without nourishment.
I have also become a reproach to them; When they see me, they shake their heads.
Assist me, Lord my God; Save me according to Your mercy.
And may they recognise that this is Your doing: You, Lord, have accomplished it.
They will curse, but You will bless; When they arise, they will be put to shame, But Your servant will rejoice.
May my accusers be clothed with disgrace, And may they cover themselves with their own shame as with a robe.
With my mouth I will give abundant thanks to the Lord, And I will praise Him among many.
For He is at the side of the needy, To deliver him from those who pass judgment on his soul.
A Psalm of David. The Lord says to my Lord: “Sit at My right hand Until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet.”
The Lord will stretch out Your strong sceptre from Zion, saying, “Rule in the midst of Your enemies.”
Your people will volunteer freely on the day of Your power; In holy splendour, from the womb of the dawn, Your youth are to You as the dew.
The Lord has made an unchangeable oath, “You are a priest forever In the manner of Melchizedek.”
The Lord stands at Your right hand; He will crush kings on the day of His anger.
He will execute judgement among the nations, Filling them with the fallen, He will crush the leaders over a vast land.
He will refresh Himself from the brook by the path; Therefore He will hold His head high.
Honour the Lord! I will express my gratitude to the Lord wholeheartedly, Amongst the upright and within the gathering.
The works of the Lord are magnificent; They are explored by all who take pleasure in them.
Splendour and majesty are His work, And His righteousness endures forever.
He has ensured His wonders are remembered; The Lord is gracious and compassionate.
He provides sustenance to those who revere Him; He will remember His covenant forever.
He has revealed to His people the might of His works, By granting them the inheritance of the nations.
The deeds of His hands are truth and justice; All His precepts are reliable.
They are steadfast for all time; They are carried out with truth and integrity.
He has brought redemption to His people; He has established His covenant forever; Holy and awe-inspiring is His name.
Reverence for the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; All those who adhere to His commandments possess good understanding; His praise endures forever.
Give praise to the Lord! Fortunate is the one who reveres the Lord, Who finds great joy in His commandments.
His descendants will be influential on the earth; The lineage of the upright will be favoured.
Wealth and riches reside in his home, And his righteousness lasts forever.
Light dawns in the darkness for the upright; He is gracious, compassionate, and just.
It goes well for a person who is generous and lends; He will uphold his cause in judgment.
For he will remain unshaken; The righteous will be remembered perpetually.
He will not dread bad news; His heart is steadfast, trusting in the Lord.
His heart is resolute, he will not fear, But will look upon his enemies with resolve.
He has given freely to the poor, His righteousness endures forever; His horn will be exalted in honour.
The wicked will see it and be troubled, He will gnash his teeth and dissolve away; The longing of the wicked will come to nothing.
Praise the Lord! Praise, you servants of the Lord, Praise the name of the Lord.
May the name of the Lord be honoured From this moment and for all time.
From sunrise to sunset, The name of the Lord is worthy of praise.
The Lord is exalted above all nations; His splendour is beyond the heavens.
Who can compare to the Lord our God, Who is seated on high,
Who humbles Himself to behold The things in heaven and on earth?
He lifts the poor from the dust, He raises the needy from the ash heap,
To place them with princes, With the leaders of His people.
He grants the childless woman a home As a joyful mother of children.
Praise the Lord! When Israel went out from Egypt, The house of Jacob from a people of a foreign language,
Judah became His holy place, Israel, His realm.
The sea observed and retreated; The Jordan flowed back.
The mountains leapt like rams, The hills, like young sheep.
What is it to you, sea, that you retreat? Jordan, that you flow back?
Mountains, that you leap like rams? Hills, like young sheep?
Quake, earth, in the presence of the Lord, In the presence of the God of Jacob,
Who transformed the rock into a pool of water, The flint into a flowing spring.
Not to us, O Lord, not to us, But to Your name be the glory, Because of Your loving kindness and Your faithfulness.
Why should the nations question, “Where is their God now?”
Yet our God is in the heavens; He accomplishes all that He desires.
Their idols are made of silver and gold, Crafted by human hands.
They have mouths, yet they do not speak; They have eyes, yet they do not see;
They have ears, yet they do not hear; They have noses, yet they do not smell;
They have hands, yet they do not feel; They have feet, yet they do not walk; They make no sound with their throat.
Those who create them will become like them, As will all who trust in them.
O Israel, place your trust in the Lord; He is your help and your shield.
House of Aaron, place your trust in the Lord; He is your help and your shield.
You who revere the Lord, place your trust in the Lord; He is your help and your shield.
The Lord is mindful of us and will bestow blessings upon us. He will bless the house of Israel; He will bless the house of Aaron.
He will bless those who revere the Lord, Both the small and the great.
May the Lord cause you to flourish, You and your children.
May you be favoured by the Lord, Creator of heaven and earth.
The heavens belong to the Lord, But the earth He has entrusted to humanity.
The dead do not extol the Lord, Nor do any who descend into silence;
But we will bless the Lord From now and forevermore.
Praise the Lord! I love the Lord, for He listens To my voice and my supplications.
Since He has turned His ear towards me, I will therefore call upon Him throughout my days.
The traps of death surrounded me And the terrors of the grave overtook me; I encountered distress and sorrow.
Then I invoked the name of the Lord: “Please, Lord, preserve my life!”
The Lord is gracious and righteous; Indeed, our God is full of compassion.
The Lord protects the simple-hearted; I was brought low, and He delivered me.
Return to your peace, my soul, For the Lord has been generous to you.
For You have rescued my soul from death, My eyes from weeping, My feet from faltering.
I will walk before the Lord In the realm of the living.
I trusted even when I said, “I am greatly afflicted.”
I declared in my dismay, “All people are deceitful.”
How can I repay the Lord For all His goodness to me?
I will raise the cup of salvation, And invoke the name of the Lord.
I will keep my promises to the Lord In the assembly of His people.
Precious in the eyes of the Lord Is the passing of His faithful ones.
Lord, I am truly Your servant; I am Your servant, the child of Your maidservant, You have freed me from my bonds.
To You I will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving, And invoke the name of the Lord.
I will keep my promises to the Lord In the assembly of His people.
In the courts of the Lord’s house, In the midst of you, Jerusalem.
Praise the Lord! Honour the Lord, all nations; Celebrate His name, all peoples!
For His kindness toward us is immense, And the faithfulness of the Lord endures forever.
Honour the Lord! Offer thanks to the Lord, for He is good; His mercy endures forever.
Let Israel declare, “His mercy endures forever.”
Let the house of Aaron declare, “His mercy endures forever.”
Let those who revere the Lord declare, “His mercy endures forever.”
In my distress, I called upon the Lord; The Lord answered me and set me in a spacious place.
The Lord is by my side; I will not fear; What can mere mortals do to me?
The Lord is with me as my helper; Therefore I will look with triumph on those who dislike me.
It is better to seek refuge in the Lord Than to place trust in mankind.
It is better to seek refuge in the Lord Than to place trust in rulers.
All nations surrounded me; In the name of the Lord, I will surely fend them off.
They encircled me, indeed, they surrounded me; In the name of the Lord, I will surely fend them off.
They swarmed around me like bees; They were extinguished like a fire among thorns; In the name of the Lord, I will surely fend them off.
You pushed me hard to make me fall, But the Lord supported me.
The Lord is my strength and my song, And He has become my deliverance.
Joyful shouts of salvation resound in the tents of the righteous; The right hand of the Lord performs valiantly.
The right hand of the Lord is lifted high; The right hand of the Lord performs valiantly.
I shall not die, but live, And proclaim the deeds of the Lord.
The Lord has disciplined me harshly, But He has not given me over to death.
Open to me the gates of righteousness; I shall enter through them and praise the Lord.
This is the gateway of the Lord; The righteous shall enter through it.
I will thank You, for You have answered me, And You have become my salvation.
The stone which the builders rejected Has become the cornerstone.
The Lord has accomplished this; It is marvellous in our eyes.
This is the day the Lord has fashioned; Let us rejoice and be glad in it.
O Lord, we beseech You, save us; O Lord, we beseech You, grant us success!
Fortunate is the one who arrives in the name of the Lord; We extend our blessings from the house of the Lord.
The Lord is God, and He has given us light; Secure the festival offering with cords to the altar's horns.
You are my God, and I will thank You; You are my God, I will exalt You.
Offer thanks to the Lord, for He is good; His mercy endures forever.
א Aleph Joyful are those whose path is pure, Who follow the Law of the Lord.
Joyful are those who observe His testimonies, And seek Him with all their heart.
They also commit no wrong; They walk in His ways.
You have established Your precepts, That we are to keep them diligently.
Oh that my ways may be steadfast To keep Your statutes!
Then I will not be ashamed When I consider all Your commandments.
I will give thanks to You with sincerity of heart, When I learn Your righteous judgments.
I will uphold Your statutes; Do not utterly abandon me! ב Beth
How can a young man keep his way pure? By keeping it according to Your word.
With all my heart I have sought You; Do not let me drift from Your commandments.
I have cherished Your word in my heart, So that I may not sin against You.
Praised are You, Lord; Teach me Your statutes.
With my lips I have declared All the ordinances of Your mouth.
I have found joy in the way of Your testimonies, As much as in all riches.
I will reflect on Your precepts And consider Your ways.
I shall take pleasure in Your statutes; I will not forget Your word. ג Gimel
Deal generously with Your servant, That I may live and keep Your word.
Open my eyes, that I may see Wonderful things from Your Law.
I am a wanderer on the earth; Do not hide Your commandments from me.
My soul is overwhelmed with longing For Your ordinances at all times.
You rebuke the proud, the cursed, Who stray from Your commandments.
Take disgrace and contempt away from me, For I observe Your testimonies.
Even though rulers sit and speak against me, Your servant reflects on Your statutes.
Your testimonies also are my delight; They are my counsellors. ד Daleth
My soul clings to the ground; Renew me according to Your word.
I have recounted my ways, and You have responded to me; Teach me Your statutes.
Help me understand the way of Your precepts, So that I will meditate on Your wonders.
My soul weeps because of sorrow; Strengthen me according to Your word.
Remove the deceitful path from me, And graciously grant me Your Law.
I have chosen the path of faithfulness; I have placed Your judgments before me.
I cling to Your testimonies; Lord, do not let me be put to shame!
I shall run the course of Your commandments, For You will enlarge my heart. ה He
Teach me, the way of Your statutes, Lord, And I shall observe it to the end.
Give me understanding, so that I may observe Your Law And keep it with all my heart.
Guide me in the path of Your commandments, For I delight in it.
Draw my heart towards Your testimonies, And not towards dishonest gain.
Turn my eyes away from looking at what is worthless, And renew me in Your ways.
Establish Your word to Your servant, As that which inspires reverence for You.
Remove my disgrace which I dread, For Your judgments are good.
Behold, I long for Your precepts; Renew me through Your righteousness. ו Waw
May Your favour also come to me, Lord, Your salvation according to Your word;
So that I will have a response for one who taunts me, For I trust in Your word.
And do not take the word of truth completely out of my mouth, For I wait for Your judgments.
So I will keep Your Law continually, For all time.
And I will walk in freedom, For I seek Your precepts.
I will also declare Your testimonies before kings, And shall not be ashamed.
I will take pleasure in Your commandments, Which I love.
And I shall lift up my hands to Your commandments, which I love; And I will reflect on Your statutes. ז Zayin
Remember the word to Your servant, In which You have made me hope.
This is my comfort in my affliction, That Your word has renewed me.
The proud utterly deride me, Yet I do not turn aside from Your Law.
I have remembered Your judgments from of old, Lord, And find comfort.
Intense indignation has seized me because of the wicked, Who abandon Your Law.
Your statutes have been my songs In the house of my journey.
Lord, I recall Your name in the night, And keep Your Law.
This has become my own, That I observe Your precepts. ח Heth
The Lord is my portion; I have promised to keep Your words.
I sought Your favour with all my heart; Be gracious to me according to Your word.
I reflected on my ways And turned my steps to Your testimonies.
I hurried and did not delay To follow Your commandments.
The traps of the wicked have surrounded me, But I have not forgotten Your Law.
I rise at midnight to give thanks to You Because of Your righteous judgments.
I am a friend to all who fear You, And to those who keep Your precepts.
The earth is full of Your mercy, Lord; Teach me Your statutes. ט Teth
You have treated Your servant well, Lord, According to Your word.
Teach me good discernment and knowledge, For I trust in Your commandments.
Before I was afflicted, I went astray, But now I always keep Your word.
You are good and do what is right; Teach me Your statutes.
The proud have forged a lie against me; With all my heart I will observe Your precepts.
Their heart is unfeeling, like hardened fat, But I delight in Your Law.
It is beneficial for me that I experienced hardship, So that I may learn Your statutes.
The Law of Your mouth is more precious to me Than thousands of gold and silver pieces. י Yodh
Your hands made me and fashioned me; Give me understanding, so that I may learn Your commandments.
May those who fear You see me and be glad, Because I hope in Your word.
I know, Lord, that Your judgments are righteous, And that You have allowed me to face trials in faithfulness.
May Your favour comfort me, According to Your word to Your servant.
May Your compassion come to me, granting me life, For Your Law is my delight.
May the proud be ashamed, for they wrong me with lies; But I shall reflect on Your precepts.
May those who fear You turn to me, And those who know Your testimonies.
May my heart be steadfast in Your statutes, So that I will not be ashamed. כ Kaph
My soul languishes for Your salvation; I wait for Your word.
My eyes fail with longing for Your word, While I say, “When will You comfort me?”
Though I have become like a dried wineskin in the smoke, I do not forget Your statutes.
What is the measure of my days, Your servant? When will You bring justice to those who pursue me?
The proud have dug pits for me, People who are not in accord with Your Law.
All Your commandments are faithful; They have persecuted me with lies; help me!
They almost destroyed me on earth, But I did not abandon Your precepts.
Revive me according to Your mercy, So that I may keep the testimony of Your mouth. ל Lamedh
Forever, Lord, Your word stands in heaven.
Your faithfulness continues throughout generations; You established the earth, and it stands.
They stand this day by Your ordinances, For all things serve You.
Had Your Law not been my delight, I would have perished of my affliction.
I will never forget Your precepts, For by them You have revived me.
I belong to You; deliver me, For I have sought Your precepts.
The wicked lie in wait to destroy me; I will diligently consider Your testimonies.
I have seen a limit to all perfection; Your commandment is exceedingly broad. מ Mem
How I love Your Law! It is my meditation all the day.
Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies, For they are ever mine.
I possess more insight than all my teachers, For Your testimonies are my meditation.
I understand more than those who are old, Because I have observed Your precepts.
I have kept my feet from every evil way, So that I may keep Your word.
I have not turned aside from Your judgments, For You have instructed me.
How sweet are Your words to my taste! Yes, sweeter than honey to my mouth!
From Your precepts I gain understanding; Therefore I hate every false way. נ Nun
Your word is as a lamp for my feet And a light on my path.
I have made an oath and will uphold it, That I will keep Your righteous judgments.
I am exceedingly afflicted; Revive me, Lord, according to Your word.
Please accept the voluntary offerings of my mouth, Lord, And teach me Your judgments.
My life is constantly in my hand, Yet I do not forget Your Law.
The wicked have set a trap for me, Yet I have not wandered from Your precepts.
I have received Your testimonies as an everlasting heritage, For they are the joy of my heart.
I have set my heart to perform Your statutes Forever, even to the end. ס Samekh
I hate those who are double-minded, But I love Your Law.
You are my refuge and my shield; I hope in Your word.
Depart from me, you evildoers, So that I may observe the commandments of my God.
Sustain me according to Your word, that I may live; And do not let me be ashamed of my hope.
Uphold me so that I may be safe, That I may have regard for Your statutes continually.
You have rejected all those who stray from Your statutes, For their deceitfulness is useless.
You have removed all the wicked of the earth as an impurity; Therefore I love Your statutes.
My flesh trembles from the fear of You, And I am afraid of Your judgments. ע Ayin
I have done justice and righteousness; Do not leave me to my oppressors.
Be a guarantor for Your servant for good; Do not let the proud oppress me.
My eyes fail with longing for Your salvation, And for Your righteous word.
Treat Your servant with Your mercy, And teach me Your statutes.
I am Your servant; grant me understanding, So that I may know Your testimonies.
It is time for the Lord to act, For they have violated Your Law.
Therefore I love Your commandments More than gold, yes, more than pure gold.
Therefore I follow all Your precepts with care, I hate every false way. פ Pe
Your testimonies are wonderful; Therefore my soul observes them.
The revelation of Your words brings light; It imparts understanding to the simple.
I opened my mouth wide with longing, For I yearned for Your commandments.
Turn to me and be gracious to me, As is fitting for those who love Your name.
Establish my footsteps in Your word, And do not let any wrongdoing have power over me.
Redeem me from human oppression, So that I may keep Your precepts.
Let Your face shine upon Your servant, And teach me Your statutes.
My eyes shed streams of water, Because they do not keep Your Law. צ Tsadhe
You are righteous, Lord, And Your judgments are right.
You have commanded Your testimonies with righteousness And in great faithfulness.
My fervour has overtaken me, Because my enemies have forgotten Your words.
Your word is very pure, Therefore Your servant loves it.
I am insignificant and despised, Yet I do not forget Your precepts.
Your righteousness is eternally just, And Your Law is the essence of truth.
Trouble and anguish have found me, Yet Your commandments bring me joy.
Your testimonies are righteous forever; Grant me understanding so that I may live. ק Qoph
I cried out with all my heart; answer me, Lord! I will observe Your statutes.
I called to You; rescue me, And I shall keep Your testimonies.
I rise before dawn and cry for help; I wait for Your words.
My eyes anticipate the night watches, So that I may meditate on Your word.
Hear my voice according to Your mercy; Revive me, Lord, according to Your judgments.
Those who pursue wickedness draw near; They are distant from Your Law.
Yet You are near, Lord, And all Your commandments are truth.
Long ago I learned from Your testimonies, That You have established them forever. ר Resh
Look at my affliction and rescue me, For I do not forget Your Law.
Plead my case and redeem me; Revive me according to Your word.
Salvation is distant from the wicked, For they do not seek Your statutes.
Great are Your mercies, Lord; Revive me according to Your judgments.
Numerous are my persecutors and my enemies, Yet I do not turn aside from Your testimonies.
I see the treacherous and feel disdain, Because they do not keep Your word.
Consider how I love Your precepts; Revive me, Lord, according to Your mercy.
The entirety of Your word is truth, And every one of Your righteous judgments is everlasting. ש Sin and Shin
Princes pursue me without reason, But my heart stands in awe of Your words.
I rejoice at Your word, Like one who finds great treasure.
I detest and abhor falsehood, But I love Your Law.
I praise You throughout the day Because of Your righteous judgments.
Those who love Your Law have great peace, And nothing causes them to stumble.
I hope for Your salvation, Lord, And follow Your commandments.
My soul keeps Your testimonies, And I love them greatly.
I observe Your precepts and Your testimonies, For all my ways are before You. ת Taw
Let my cry come before You, Lord; Give me understanding according to Your word.
Let my pleading come before You; Save me according to Your word.
Let my lips overflow with praise, For You teach me Your statutes.
Let my tongue sing of Your word, For all Your commandments are righteousness.
Let Your hand be prepared to assist me, For I have chosen Your precepts.
I yearn for Your salvation, Lord, And Your Law is my delight.
Let my soul live that it may praise You, And let Your ordinances assist me.
I have wandered about like a lost sheep; seek Your servant, For I do not forget Your commandments.
A Song of Ascents. In my trouble I cried to the Lord, And He answered me.
Lord, save my soul from lying lips, From a deceitful tongue.
What will be given to you, and what more will be done to you, You deceitful tongue?
Keen arrows of the warrior, Along with the glowing coals of the broom tree!
Alas for me, for I dwell in Meshech, And I have made my home among the tents of Kedar!
My soul has dwelt too long Among those who are against peace.
I seek peace, but when I speak, They pursue conflict.
A Song of Ascents. I lift my eyes up to the mountains; From where will my help come?
My help comes from the Lord, Creator of heaven and earth.
He will not let your foot falter; The one who watches over you will not slumber.
Behold, the guardian of Israel Will neither slumber nor sleep.
The Lord is your keeper; The Lord provides shade at your right hand.
And the sun will not harm you by day, Nor the moon by night.
The Lord will shield you from all harm; He will preserve your soul.
The Lord will watch over your coming and going Now and forevermore.
A Song of Ascents. Of David. I rejoiced when they said to me, “Let us go to the dwelling of the Lord.”
Our feet are now standing Within your gates, O Jerusalem,
Jerusalem, that is built As a city that is joined together;
To which the tribes ascend, the tribes of the Lord— A decree for Israel— To give thanks to the name of the Lord.
For seats of judgment were established there, The seats of the house of David.
Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: “May those who cherish you find safety.
May tranquillity be within your walls, And prosperity within your palaces.”
For the sake of my kin and companions, I will now say, “May tranquillity be within you.”
For the sake of the house of the Lord our God, I will pursue your welfare.
A Song of Ascents. To You I have raised my eyes, You who are enthroned in the heavens!

Observe, just as the eyes of servants are fixed on the hand of their master, And as the eyes of a maid look to the hand of her mistress, So our eyes are directed to the Lord our God, Until He shows us His favour.
Show us Your kindness, Lord, show us Your kindness, For we have endured more than enough of contempt.
Our soul has endured more than enough Of the scoffing of those who are at ease, And of the contempt of the proud.
A Song of Ascents. Of David. “Had it not been the Lord who was on our side,” Let Israel now say,
“If the Lord had not been on our side When people rose up against us,
Then they would have devoured us completely, When their anger burned fiercely against us;
Then the waters would have flooded over us, The stream would have swept over our soul;
and the raging waters would have swept over our soul.”
Praised be the Lord, Who has not allowed us to be torn by their teeth.
Our soul has escaped like a bird from the trapper’s snare; The snare is broken and we have escaped.
Our aid is in the name of the Lord, Who created heaven and earth.
A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the Lord Are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved but remains forever.
Just as the mountains encircle Jerusalem, So the Lord encircles His people Now and for all time.
For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest upon the land of the righteous, So that the righteous will not extend their hands to do wrong.
Show kindness, Lord, to those who are good, And to those who are upright in their hearts.
But for those who turn aside to their crooked ways, The Lord will guide them away with those who practice injustice.
May peace be upon Israel. A Song of Ascents. When the Lord restored the fortunes of Zion, We were like those who dream.
Then our mouth was filled with laughter And our tongue with songs of joy; Then they said among the nations, “The Lord has done great things for them.”
The Lord has done great things for us; We are filled with joy.
Renew our fortunes, Lord, Like the streams in the Negev.
Those who sow in tears shall reap with songs of joy.
One who goes forth weeping, carrying seed to sow, Shall indeed return with shouts of joy, bringing his sheaves with him.
A Song of Ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the Lord builds the house, They who build it, labour in vain; Unless the Lord guards the city, The watchman stays awake in vain.
It is futile for you to rise up early, To stay up late, To eat the bread of painful labours; For He gives to His beloved even in his sleep.
Observe, children are a blessing from the Lord, The fruit of the womb is a gracious reward.
Just as arrows are in the hand of a warrior, So are the children born in one's youth.
Blessed is the man whose quiver is filled; They will not be put to shame When they engage with their adversaries at the gate.
A Song of Ascents. Blessed is everyone who fears the Lord, Who walks in His ways.
You will eat the fruit of the labour of your hands; You will be happy, and it will go well for you.
Your wife will be like a fruitful vine within your home, Your children like young olive shoots around your table.
Observe, for in this manner shall a man Who reveres the Lord be favoured.
May the Lord bless you from Zion, And may you witness the flourishing of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
Truly, may you see your descendants.
Peace be upon Israel! A Song of Ascents. “Many times they have attacked me from my youth up,” Let Israel say,
“Many times they have attacked me since my youth; Yet they have not overcome me.
The ploughers ploughed upon my back; They lengthened their furrows.”
The Lord is just; He has severed the cords of the wicked.
May all who despise Zion Be put to shame and turned back;
May they be like grass on the housetops, Which withers before it grows up;
With which the reaper does not fill his hand, Nor does the binder of sheaves fill his arms;
Nor do those who pass by say, “May the Lord's blessing be upon you; We bless you in the Lord's name.”
A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to You, Lord.
Lord, listen to my voice! May Your ears be attentive To the sound of my supplications.
If You, Lord, were to mark our iniquities, Lord, who could stand?
But with You there is forgiveness, Therefore You are revered.
I wait for the Lord, my soul is patient, And I place my hope in His word.
My soul waits eagerly for the Lord More than the watchmen wait for the morning; Indeed, more than the watchmen for the morning.
Israel, place your hope in the Lord; For with the Lord there is lovingkindness, And with Him is plentiful redemption.
And He will deliver Israel From all his iniquities.
A Song of Ascents. Of David. Lord, my heart is not proud, nor my eyes arrogant; Nor do I involve myself in great matters, Or in things too difficult for me.
I have indeed calmed and quieted my soul, Like a weaned child resting with its mother, My soul within me is like a weaned child.
Israel, wait for the Lord From now and forever.
A Song of Ascents. Remember, Lord, in David’s behalf, All his affliction;
How he made an oath to the Lord And pledged to the Mighty One of Jacob:
“I surely will not enter my house, Nor lie down on my bed;
I will not allow sleep to my eyes, Or slumber to my eyelids,
Until I find a place for the Lord, A sanctuary for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
Indeed, we heard of it in Ephrathah, We discovered it in the fields of Jaar.
Let us enter His dwelling place; Let us bow at His footstool.
Rise, Lord, to Your resting place, You and the ark of Your might.
May Your priests be adorned with righteousness, And may Your faithful ones shout for joy.
For the sake of Your servant David, Do not reject the face of Your anointed.
The Lord has made a promise to David A truth He will not revoke: “I will place upon your throne One from your own lineage.
If your sons will uphold My covenant And the decrees I will teach them, Their descendants will also sit upon your throne forever.”
For the Lord has chosen Zion; He has wished for it as His dwelling.
“This is My resting place forever; Here I will reside, for I have longed for it.
I will greatly bless her provisions; I will satisfy her poor with bread.
I will also clothe her priests with salvation, And her faithful will sing joyfully.
I will cause the horn of David to flourish there; I have prepared a light for My anointed.
I will cover his enemies with disgrace, But upon him, his crown will shine.”
A Song of Ascents. Of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is For brothers to dwell together in unity!
It is like the precious oil on the head, Running down upon the beard, Aaron’s beard, Coming down upon the edge of his robes.
It is like the dew of Hermon Coming down upon the mountains of Zion; For there the Lord commanded the blessing— Life forever.
A Song of Ascents. Behold, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, Who serve by night in the house of the Lord!
Raise your hands towards the holy place And offer praise to the Lord.
May the Lord, the Creator of heaven and earth, bless you from Zion.
Praise the Lord! Honour the name of the Lord; Praise Him, you servants of the Lord,
You who stand in the dwelling of the Lord, In the courtyards of the house of our God!
Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good; Sing praises to His name, for it is delightful.
For the Lord has chosen Jacob for His own, Israel as a cherished possession.
For I am certain that the Lord is great, And that our Lord is above all other gods.
Whatever the Lord desires, He accomplishes, In heaven and on earth, in the seas and in all the ocean depths.
He causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth, Who makes lightning for the rain, Who brings forth the wind from His storehouses.
He struck the firstborn of Egypt, Both of man and animal.
He sent signs and wonders into your midst, Egypt, Against Pharaoh and all his servants.
He defeated many nations And brought down mighty kings:
Sihon, ruler of the Amorites, And Og, ruler of Bashan, And all the territories of Canaan;
And He granted their land as an inheritance, An inheritance to His people Israel.
Your name, Lord, is everlasting, Your remembrance, Lord, is throughout all generations.
For the Lord will judge His people And will show compassion on His servants.
The idols of the nations are crafted from silver and gold, The work of human hands.
They have mouths, yet they do not speak; They have eyes, yet they do not see;
They have ears, yet they do not hear, Nor is there any breath in their mouths.
Those who make them will become like them, Everyone who trusts in them.
House of Israel, honour the Lord; House of Aaron, honour the Lord;
House of Levi, honour the Lord; You who revere the Lord, honour the Lord.
Blessed be the Lord, who comes from Zion, Who dwells in Jerusalem.
Praise the Lord! Give thanks to the Lord, He is good, His love endures forever.
Give thanks to Him, the God of gods, His love endures forever.
Give thanks to Him, the Lord of lords, His love endures forever.
To Him who alone performs great wonders, His love endures forever.
To Him who made the heavens with skill, His love endures forever.
To Him who spread out the earth above the waters, His love endures forever.
To Him who made the great lights, His love endures forever.
The sun to rule by day, His love endures forever.
The moon and stars to rule by night, His love endures forever.
To Him who struck the Egyptians, that is, their firstborn, His love endures forever.
And brought Israel out from their midst, His love endures forever.
With a strong hand and an outstretched arm, His love endures forever.
To Him who divided the Red Sea in parts, His love endures forever.
And led Israel through the midst of it, His love endures forever.
But He overthrew Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea, His love endures forever.
To Him who guided His people through the wilderness, His love endures forever.
To Him who struck great kings, His love endures forever.
And brought death to mighty kings, His love endures forever.
Sihon, the ruler of the Amorites, His love endures forever.
And Og, the ruler of Bashan, His love endures forever.
And granted their land as a heritage, His love endures forever.
A heritage to His servant Israel, His love endures forever.
Who remembered us in our lowliness, His love endures forever.
And has rescued us from our enemies, His love endures forever.
Who gives food to all flesh, His love endures forever.
Offer gratitude to the God of heaven, His love endures forever.
By the rivers of Babylon, There we sat and wept, And we remembered Zion.
On the willows in its midst We placed our harps.
For there our captors asked us for songs, And our tormentors for joy, saying, “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
How can we sing the Lord’s song While in a foreign land?
If I forget you, Jerusalem, May my right hand lose its skill.
May my tongue adhere to the roof of my mouth If I fail to remember you, If I do not honour Jerusalem Above my greatest joy.
Recall, Lord, against the sons of Edom The day of Jerusalem, Those who said, “Tear it down, tear it down To its very foundation!”
Daughter of Babylon, you devastated one, Blessed will be the one who repays you With the recompense you have given us.
Blessed is he then, who would seize your children and throw them dashed on your rocks.
A Psalm of David. I will give You thanks with all my heart; I will sing Your praises before the gods.
I will bow in reverence toward Your sacred temple And give thanks to Your name for Your mercy and Your truth; For You have magnified Your word in accordance with all Your name.
On the day I called, You heard me; You infused my soul with courage and strength.
All the kings of the earth shall give thanks to You, Lord, When they have heard the words spoken by Your mouth.
And they shall sing of the paths of the Lord, For the glory of the Lord is indeed great.
For the Lord is exalted, Yet He regards the lowly, But the haughty He knows from afar.
Even though I walk amidst trouble, You will revive me; You will extend Your hand against the anger of my enemies, And Your right hand will deliver me.
The Lord will fulfil His purpose for me; Your mercy, Lord, is everlasting; Do not abandon the works of Your hands.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. Lord, You have searched me and know me.
You know when I sit down and when I rise up; You perceive my thoughts from a distance.
You scrutinise my path and my lying down, And are acquainted with all my ways.
Even before a word is on my tongue, Behold, Lord, You know it completely.
You hem me in Lord, behind and in front, And laid Your hand upon me.
Such knowledge is beyond my understanding; It is so lofty, I cannot grasp it.
Where could I go from Your Spirit? Or where could I escape from Your presence?
If I go up to heaven, You are there; If I lie myself in the depths, You are there.
If I take up the wings of the dawn, And dwell in the farthest part of the sea,
Even there Your hand will lead me, And Your right hand will hold me tight.
If I say, “Surely darkness will cover me, And the light around me will become night,”
Even darkness is not dark to You, And the night shines like the day. Darkness and light are both alike to You.
For You formed my innermost being; You knit me together in my mother’s womb.
I will thank You, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; Wonderful are Your works, And my soul knows it very well.
My frame was not hidden from You When I was made in secret, And skilfully formed in the depths of the earth.
Your eyes saw my unformed body; And in Your book were written All the days that were planned for me, When none of them had yet come to be.
How treasured are Your thoughts to me, God! How vast is the sum of them!
If I were to count them, they would outnumber the grains of sand. When I awake, I am still with You.
If only You would slay the wicked, God; Depart from me, you men of bloodshed.
For they speak against You with malice, And Your adversaries misuse Your name.
Do I not hate those who hate You, Lord? And do I not despise those who rise up against You?
I hate them with complete hatred; They have become my foes.
Examine me, God, and know my heart; Test me and know my anxious thoughts;
And see if there is any offensive way in me, And lead me in the way everlasting.
For the music director. A Psalm of David. Rescue me, Lord, from evil people; Protect me from violent men
Who plot evil schemes in their hearts; They are constantly stirring conflicts.
They sharpen their tongues like a serpent; The venom of a viper is under their lips. Selah
Keep me, Lord, from the hands of the wicked; Protect me from violent men Who intend to trip up my steps.
The proud have laid a trap for me, with snares; They have spread a net beside the path; They have set traps for me. Selah
I declared to the Lord, “You are my God; Hear, Lord, the sound of my plea.”
Sovereign Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have shielded my head on the day of battle.
Do not grant, Lord, the desires of the wicked; Do not let their wicked plans succeed, so that they are not exalted. Selah
As for those who surround me, May their own words bring them harm.
May burning coals fall upon them; May they be thrown into the fire, Into deep pits from which they cannot escape.
May a slanderer not endure on the earth; May evil pursue a violent man to his ruin.
I am confident that the Lord will uphold the cause of the afflicted, And bring justice to the poor.
Surely the righteous will offer thanks to Your name; The upright will abide in Your presence.
A Psalm of David. Lord, I call upon You; hurry to me! Listen to my voice when I call to You!
Let my prayer rise before You like incense; The lifting up of my hands like the evening sacrifice.
Place a guard, Lord, at my mouth; Watch over the entrance of my lips.
Do not let my heart be drawn to anything evil, To engage in acts of wickedness With those who commit wrong; And may I not partake in their delicacies.
Let the righteous strike me, it is a kindness; Let him rebuke me, it is oil for the head; My head shall not refuse it, For still my prayer is against their evil deeds.
Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock, And they will hear my words, for they are pleasant.
As when one ploughs and breaks open the earth, Our bones have been scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
For my eyes are toward You, God the Lord; In You I take refuge, do not leave my soul defenceless.
Protect me from the jaws of the trap they have set for me, And from the snares of those who do evil.
Let the wicked fall into their own nets, While I pass by unharmed.
A Maskil of David. When he was in the cave. A Prayer. I cry out with my voice to the Lord; I plead with my voice to the Lord for mercy.
I pour out my distress before Him; I reveal my troubles to Him.
When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, You were aware of my going. On the path where I walk They have set a snare for me.
Look to the right and observe; For there is no one who notices me. There is no escape for me; No one is concerned for my soul.
I called out to You, Lord; I declared, “You are my refuge, My share in the land of the living.
Listen to my cry, For I have been brought very low; Deliver me from my persecutors, For they are too powerful for me.
Raise my soul up from sorrow, So that I may give thanks to Your name; The righteous will gather around me, For You will care for me.”
A Psalm of David. Hear my prayer, Lord, Listen to my pleadings! Answer me in Your faithfulness, in Your righteousness!
And do not make Your servant stand trial, For no one alive is righteous before You.
For the enemy has pursued my soul; He has brought my life low to the ground; He has made me dwell in darkness, like those long deceased.
Therefore my spirit is faint within me; My heart is dismayed within me.
I remember the days of old; I meditate on all Your doings; I reflect on the work of Your hands.
I reach out my hands to You; My soul thirsts for You, like a parched land. Selah
Respond to me swiftly, Lord, My spirit is failing; Do not conceal Your face from me, Or I will be like those who descend to the pit.
Let me hear of Your mercy in the morning, For I trust in You; Teach me the way in which I should walk, For to You I lift up my soul.
Rescue me, Lord, from my enemies; I seek shelter in You.
Guide me to do Your will, For You are my God; Let Your good Spirit guide me on level ground.
For the sake of Your name, Lord, give me life. In Your righteousness deliver my soul from distress.
And in Your mercy, vanquish my enemies, And destroy all those who oppress my soul, For I am Your servant.
A Psalm of David. Blessed be the Lord, my rock, Who trains my hands for war, And my fingers for battle;
My faithfulness and my fortress, My stronghold and my saviour, My shield and He in whom I take refuge, Who subdues my people under me.
Lord, what is man, that You care for him? Or the children of man, that You are mindful of them?
Man is like a breath; His days are like a fleeting shadow.
Lower Your heavens, Lord, and descend; Touch the mountains, so they may smoke.
Send forth lightning and scatter them; Release Your arrows and disorient them.
Extend Your hand from above; Deliver me and save me from mighty waters, From the grasp of strangers
Whose mouths speak deceit, And whose right hand is a hand of falsehood.
O God, I will sing a new song to You; With a harp of ten strings I will offer praises to You,
Who grants deliverance to kings, Who saves His servant David from the harmful sword.
Deliver me and save me from the grasp of strangers, Whose mouths speak deceit, And whose right hand is a hand of falsehood.
May our sons in their youth be like thriving plants, And our daughters like corner pillars Crafted for a palace.
May our granaries be full, Providing every kind of produce, May our flocks bring forth thousands And ten thousands in our fields;
May our cattle be bred Without mishap and without loss, May there be no distress in our streets!
Blessed are the people who are in such a state; Blessed are the people whose God is the Lord!
A Psalm of Praise by David. I will exalt You, my God, the King, And I will bless Your name forever and ever.
Each day I will bless You, And I will praise Your name for all time.
The Lord is great and worthy of high praise, And His greatness cannot be fathomed.
One generation will commend Your works to another, And will proclaim Your mighty acts.
On the glorious splendour of Your majesty And on Your wonderful works, I will meditate.
People will speak of the power of Your awe-inspiring acts, And I will recount Your greatness.
They will burst forth in speaking of the memory of Your abundant goodness, And will shout joyfully of Your righteousness.
The Lord is gracious and full of compassion; Slow to anger and rich in mercy.
The Lord is kind to all, And His mercies extend over all His works.
All Your works will give thanks to You, Lord, And Your faithful ones will bless You.
They will speak of the glory of Your kingdom, And discuss Your might,
To make known to the children of mankind Your mighty acts, And the glory of the majesty of Your kingdom.
Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, And Your dominion endures throughout all generations. The Lord is trustworthy in all he promises and faithful in all he does
The Lord upholds all who stumble, And lifts up all who are bowed down.
The eyes of all look to You, And You provide them with their food at the proper time.
You open Your hand And satisfy the needs of every living thing.
The Lord is righteous in all His ways, And gracious in all His works.
The Lord is near to all who call upon Him, To all who call upon Him sincerely.
He will fulfill the desire of those who revere Him; He will also hear their cry for help and rescue them.
The Lord protects all who love Him, But He will bring an end to all the wicked.
My mouth will declare the praise of the Lord, And all people will bless His holy name for all eternity.
Praise the Lord! Honour the Lord, my soul!
I shall praise the Lord as long as I live; I will sing to my God for as long as I exist.
Place no trust in noblemen, In mortal man, where there is no deliverance.
When his spirit departs, he returns to the earth; On that very day, his plans come to nothing.
Fortunate is he whose aid is the God of Jacob, Whose hope rests in the Lord, his God,
Who created heaven and earth, The sea and all that is within them; Who remains faithful forever;
Who brings justice to the oppressed; Who provides food for the hungry. The Lord releases the captives.
The Lord gives sight to the blind; The Lord lifts up those who are bowed down; The Lord cherishes the righteous.
The Lord protects strangers; He upholds the orphan and the widow, But He frustrates the plans of the wicked.
The Lord shall reign eternally, Your God, Zion, through all generations.
Honour the Lord! Praise the Lord! It is good to sing praises to our God; It is delightful, and such praise is lovely.
The Lord restores Jerusalem; He gathers the exiles of Israel.
He mends the broken-hearted And heals their wounds.
He numbers the stars; He names each one.
Great is our Lord, and mighty in power; His understanding knows no bounds.
The Lord upholds the oppressed; He brings the wicked to the ground.
Offer songs to the Lord with gratitude; Play music to our God on the lyre,
Who spreads clouds across the sky, Who sends rain upon the earth, Who causes grass to flourish on the mountains.
He provides food for the animals, And to the young ravens when they call.
He finds no joy in the strength of the horse; He takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.
The Lord delights in those who revere Him, Those who hope in His mercy.
Praise the Lord, Jerusalem! Honour your God, Zion!
For He has fortified the bars of your gates; He has blessed your children within you.
He grants peace within your borders; He fills you with the finest wheat.
He sends His command to the earth; His word moves swiftly.
He bestows snow like wool; He spreads frost like ashes.
He casts His ice like fragments; Who can withstand His cold?
He sends His word and melts them; He makes His wind blow, and the waters flow.
He reveals His words to Jacob, His statutes and judgments to Israel.
He has not acted this way with any other nation; And as for His judgments, they have not understood them.
Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord! Glorify the Lord from the heavens; Praise Him in the heights!
Praise Him, all His angels; Glorify Him, all His heavenly hosts!
Praise Him, sun and moon; Glorify Him, all shining stars!
Praise Him, you highest heavens, And the waters above the heavens!
Let them praise the name of the Lord, For He commanded, and they came into being.
He has established them forever and ever; He has made a decree, and it will not pass away.
Glorify the Lord from the earth, Sea creatures and all the ocean depths;
Fire and hail, snow and mist; Stormy wind, carrying out His word;
The towering mountains and gentle hills; Fruit-bearing trees and majestic cedars;
Animals and all livestock; Creeping things and flying birds;
The Kings of the earth and all peoples; All who rule and all judge on the earth;
Both young men and maidens; The old and the young.
Let them praise the name of the Lord, For His name alone is exalted; His splendour is above earth and heaven.
And He has raised a horn for His people, Praise for all His faithful ones, For the children of Israel, a people close to Him.
Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord! Sing a fresh song to the Lord, And celebrate Him in the congregation of the faithful.
Israel shall delight in their Creator; The children of Zion shall exult in their Sovereign.
They shall praise His name with dancing; They shall sing to Him with tambourine and lyre.
For the Lord delights in His people; He will adorn the humble with salvation.
The faithful shall be joyful in glory; They shall sing for joy upon their beds.
The exalted praises of God shall be in their mouths, And a double-edged sword in their hands,
To bring justice to the nations, And correction to the peoples,
To bind their rulers with chains, And their nobles with shackles of iron,
To carry out the judgement decreed against them.
This honour is for all His faithful ones. Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord! Honour God in His holy place; Praise Him in His vast heavens.
Honour Him for His mighty acts; Praise Him for His supreme greatness.
Honour Him with the sound of the trumpet; Praise Him with harp and lyre.
Honour Him with tambourine and dance; Praise Him with stringed instruments and flute.
Honour Him with loud cymbals; Praise Him with resounding cymbals.
Everything that has breath, praise the Lord. Praise the Lord!
The wise sayings of Solomon, son of David, ruler of Israel:
To gain wisdom and instruction, to discern words of understanding;
To receive instruction in wise conduct, in righteousness, justice, and fairness;
To impart prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the young;
Let the wise hear and increase their learning, and let those of understanding acquire guidance,
To comprehend proverbs and parables, the sayings and enigmas of the wise.
For the fear of the Lord is the start of all knowledge; those who are foolish hate wisdom and instruction.
Pay attention, my son, to your father’s guidance, and do not abandon your mother’s teaching;
For they are like a beautiful crown for your head and ornaments for your neck.
My son, if sinners try to lure you, do not follow.
If they say, “Join us, let us wait in secret for blood, let us ambush the innocent without cause;
Let us swallow them alive like the grave, and whole, like those who go down to the pit;
We shall find all kinds of precious goods, we shall fill our houses with plunder;
Throw in your lot with us, we shall all share one purse”—
My son, do not walk in their way, keep your foot from their path,
For they are swift to pursue wrongdoing, and they hasten to shed blood.
How futile it is to spread a net in full view of all the birds!
But these men are setting a trap for themselves; they ambush their own lives.
Such are the ways of everyone who is greedy for unjust gain; it takes away the life of those who pursue it.
Wisdom calls aloud in the street, she raises her voice in the public squares;
At the head of the bustling streets she cries out, at the entrance of the city gates she makes her speech:
“How long, you simple ones, will you love your simple ways? How long will scoffers delight in scoffing and fools hate knowledge?
Repent at my rebuke! Then I will pour out my spirit upon you, I will make my words known to you.
Because I have called and you refused, I have stretched out my hand and no one heeded;
Since you ignored all my advice and would not accept my rebuke,
I will also smile at your calamity; I will respond with mirth when your dread comes,
When your dread comes like a storm and your calamity like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come upon you.
They will call upon me, but I will not respond; they will seek me earnestly but will not find me,
Because they rejected wisdom and did not choose to fear the Lord.
They would not accept my advice and spurned all my rebuke.
Therefore they shall eat the fruit of their ways and be filled with the fruit of their schemes.
For the waywardness of the simple will lead to their downfall, and the complacency of fools will bring about their ruin;
But whoever listens to me will dwell securely and will be at ease, without fear of harm.”
My son, if you accept my words and treasure my commandments within you,
So listen closely to wisdom and open your heart to understanding—
Indeed, if you cry out for insight and raise your voice for understanding,
If you seek it as silver and search for it as for hidden treasure,
Then you will grasp what it means to revere the Lord and find the knowledge of God.
For the Lord grants wisdom; from His mouth flow knowledge and understanding.
He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to those whose walk is blameless,
Guarding the paths of justice and watching over the way of His faithful ones.
Then you will perceive what is right and just and fair—every good path.
For wisdom will fill your heart, and knowledge will bring joy to your soul.
Discretion will protect you, and understanding will watch over you.
To save you from the ways of wicked men, from those who speak perverse things,
From those who have left the paths of uprightness to walk in dark ways,
Who delight in doing wrong and rejoice in the perversity of evil,
Whose ways are twisted and who are deceitful in their actions.
To save you from the adulterous woman, from the foreign woman who flatters with her words,
Who leaves the partner of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God.
For her house leads down to death, and her paths to the spirits of the dead.
None who go to her return, nor do they attain the paths of life.
So you will walk in the ways of good people and keep to the paths of the righteous.
For those who are upright will inhabit the land, and those who are blameless will dwell in it;
But the wicked will be removed from the land, and the treacherous will be uprooted from it.
My son, do not forget my teaching, but let your heart comply with my commandments;
for they will add length of days and years of life, and bring you peace.
Let kindness and truth never leave you; bind them around your neck, inscribe them on the tablet of your heart.
Thus you will find favour and a good reputation in the sight of God and man.
Trust fully in the Lord, with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding.
In all your ways acknowledge Him, and He shall make your paths straight.
Do not consider yourself wise; fear the Lord and turn from from evil.
This will bring well-being to your body and refreshment to your bones.
Give honour to the Lord with your wealth, and with the first-fruits of all your produce;
then your barns will be filled with abundance, and your vats will overflow with new wine.
My son, do not reject the Lord’s discipline, or loathe His rebuke,
for the Lord disciplines those He loves, just as a father disciplines the son in whom he delights.
Blessed is the one who finds wisdom, and the one who gains understanding,
for her profit is better than silver, and her yield better than gold.
She is more valuable than jewels, and nothing you desire can equal her.
A long life accompanies her in one hand; in the other are wealth and honour.
Her ways are pleasant, and all her paths are peace.
She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her; happy are those who hold her fast.
By wisdom the Lord founded the earth; by understanding He established the heavens.
By His knowledge the depths were divided, and the clouds drop the dew.
My son, do not let wisdom and discretion escape from your sight; preserve sound judgment,
and they will be life to your soul and an adornment to your neck.
Then you will walk securely on your way, and your foot will not stumble.
When you lie down, you will not be afraid; your sleep will be sweet.
Do not fear sudden disaster, nor the ruin of the wicked when it comes;
for the Lord will be your confidence and will prevent your foot from being ensnared.
Do not hold back good from those who deserve it, when it is within your power to help.
Do not say to your neighbour, “Go, and come back, and tomorrow I will give it to you,” when you have it with you.
Do not plot harm against your neighbour, who lives trustfully beside you.
Do not contend with anyone without cause, if they have done you no harm.
Do not be envious of those who are violent, and do not choose any of their ways.
For the devious are detestable to the Lord, but He is intimate with the upright.
The Lord’s curse is upon the dwelling of the wicked, but He blesses the home of the righteous.
He mocks the mockers, but gives grace to the humble.
The wise will inherit honour, but fools will only gain disgrace.
Hear, my sons, a father's instruction; pay heed to gain understanding.
For I offer you sound teaching; do not forsake my guidance.
When I was a son to my father, tender and cherished by my mother,
He instructed me and said, “Let your heart grasp my words; keep my commandments and live.
Seek wisdom, seek understanding; do not forget nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
Do not abandon wisdom, and she will protect you; love her, and she will watch over you.
The beginning of wisdom is this: seek wisdom. Though it costs all you have, gain understanding.
Value her, and she will exalt you; embrace her, and she will honour you.
She will place a garland of grace on your head and present you with a crown of splendour.”
Hear, my son, accept my sayings, and the years of your life will be many.
I guide you in the way of wisdom and lead you in upright paths.
When you walk, your steps will not be hindered; and if you run, you will not stumble.
Hold fast to instruction, do not let it go; guard it, for it is your life.
Do not enter the path of the wicked or walk in the way of evildoers.
Avoid it, do not travel on it; turn from it and continue on your way.
For they cannot sleep unless they do evil; they are robbed of slumber until they cause someone to stumble.
They consume the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
But the path of the righteous is like the morning light, growing brighter until the full light of day.
The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know what makes them stumble.
My son, heed my words; listen carefully to my sayings.
Keep them always in your sight; hold them close within your heart.
For they are life to those who find them and health to the whole body.
Protect your heart with all diligence, for it is the wellspring of life.
Rid yourself of a deceitful mouth; keep corrupt speech far from your lips.
Let your eyes look directly ahead; fix your gaze straight before you.
Ponder the path of your feet, and all your ways will be secure.
Do not turn to the right or to the left; keep your foot from evil.
My son, pay attention to my wisdom, and incline your ear to my understanding,
so that you may maintain discretion, and your lips may preserve knowledge.
For the lips of an adulterous woman drip honey, and her speech is smoother than oil;
but ultimately she is as bitter as wormwood, as sharp as a double-edged sword.
Her feet descend to death, her steps lead straight to Sheol.
She does not consider the path of life; her ways are unstable, and she does not know it.
Therefore, my sons, listen to me and do not turn away from my words.
Keep your path far from her, and do not approach the door of her house,
lest you give your honour to others, and your years to the cruel one;
lest strangers be filled with your wealth, and your hard-earned possessions go to the house of a foreigner;
and in the end, you will groan when your flesh and body are spent,
You will say, “How I hated discipline, and my heart rejected correction!
I did not heed the voice of my teachers, nor did I listen to my instructors.
I was almost in complete ruin in the midst of the assembly and congregation.”
Only drink water from your own storage jars, and only draw your fresh water from your own wells.
Should your springs overflow into the streets, your streams of water in the public squares?
Let them be yours alone, not to be shared with strangers.
May your source of joy be blessed, and find delight in the wife of your youth.
A loving doe, a graceful gazelle—may her breasts satisfy you always, may you be ever intoxicated with her love.
Why, my son, should you be intoxicated with an adulteress, and embrace the bosom of a wayward woman?
For the ways of everyone are before the eyes of the Lord, and He examines all their paths.
The wrongdoings of the wicked will ensnare them, and they will be held fast by the cords of their sins.
They will die for lack of discipline, and in the greatness of their folly they will go astray.
My son, if you have become a guarantor for your neighbour, or have given a handshake for a stranger,
you have been ensnared by the words of your mouth, caught by what you have spoken.
So do this, my son, to save yourself, since you have fallen into your neighbour's hands: Go, humble yourself, and be urgent with your neighbour to free yourself.
Do not allow sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids;
Save yourself like a gazelle from the hunter's hand, and like a bird from the snare of the fowler.
Consider the ant, you lazy one; watch its ways and gain wisdom!
It has no chief, officer, or ruler,
yet it prepares its food in the summer and gathers its provision at harvest.
How long will you lie there, you lazy one? When will you arise from your sleep?
A brief sleep, a little slumber, a slight folding of the hands to rest—
and poverty will come upon you like a drifter, and scarcity like an armed man.
A worthless and wicked person, who walks with a corrupt mouth,
who winks with his eyes, signals with his feet, and points with his fingers,
who plots evil with deceit in his heart— he continually spreads strife.
Therefore disaster will overtake him suddenly; instantly he will be broken without remedy.
There are six things the Lord hates, seven that are an abomination to Him:
proud eyes, a deceitful tongue, and hands that spill innocent blood,
a heart that plans wicked schemes, feet that are swift to rush into evil,
a false witness who pours out lies, and one who spreads strife among brothers.
My son, keep your father's commandment, and do not forsake your mother's teaching.
Bind them continually on your heart; fasten them around your neck.
When you walk, they will guide you; when you sleep, they will watch over you; and when you awake, they will speak to you.
For the commandment is a lamp, the teaching is a light, and corrections for discipline are the way to life,
to keep you from the evil woman, from the smooth tongue of the wayward woman.
Do not desire her beauty in your heart, nor let her captivate you with her eyes.
For a prostitute can be had for a loaf of bread, but an adulteress hunts for a precious life.
Can a man take fire into his lap without his clothes being burned?
Or can one walk on hot coals without his feet being scorched?
So is the one who goes in to his neighbour's wife; whoever touches her will not go unpunished.
A thief is not despised if he steals to satisfy his hunger when he is starving;
yet if he is caught, he must repay sevenfold, even if it costs him all the wealth of his house.
But one who commits adultery with a woman lacks sense; he who does so destroys himself.
He will face wounds and disgrace, and his shame will never be wiped away.
